question: I can see the Unlimited 5G Data Pack on my mobile number but why am I not able to use 5G on my iPhone?
answer: We have provided the Jio Welcome Offer on your number to enable unlimited 5G usage. Start using Jio True 5G by updating the device software to iOS 16.4 or later versions. For that, simply go to ‘Settings’, select ‘General’ and click on 'Software Update'. In case you are using 2 SIM cards in your iPhone: Turn on the ‘5G Auto’ mode from ‘Settings’ following the below steps Go to ‘Settings’ Select ‘Mobile Data’ and tap on ‘Jio SIM’ Tap on ‘Voice & Data’ and select ‘5G Auto’ In case you are using only 1 SIM in your iPhone: Turn on the ‘5G Auto’ mode from ‘Settings’ following the below steps Go to ‘Settings’ and select ‘Mobile Data’ Tap on ‘Mobile Data Option’ Choose ‘Voice & Data’ and turn on ‘5G Auto’

question: Are these Jio ALL-IN-ONE PLANS applicable for JioPhone customers?
answer: Yes, Jio Phone customers are also eligible for these plans. However, they will have to pay for prime membership of Rs.99.

question: Will I get voice SMS benefits in Jio Data Add-On?
answer: There are no SMS / Voice benefits in Data Add-On. This data pack allows you to use data only.

question: How do I enrol for Jio Prime?
answer: No action is required at your end. All Jio subscribers onboarded on or after 25th November 2024 will get the subscription at no extra cost along with the first recharge or enrolling for a postpaid plan. All existing smartphone customers are already enroled for Jio Prime membership and will continue to enjoy the benefits as long as they are on an active Jio recharges or postpaid plan.

question: Are there any charges for calling to Non-Jio numbers?
answer: No, there are no charges for calling to Non-Jio numbers

question: I want to enrol for Jio Prime, can you share more details?
answer: Jio Prime is an exclusive membership program which will ensure that PRIME members get substantially better benefits than the counterparts in the industry. In the past years, PRIME customers have already experienced: Best Tariffs in the industry. Complimentary access to best content library (TV channels, movies, videos, songs, magazine, newspapers and so on.). MyJio – The digital gateway (single access to 24X7 self-service & account management, access to all JioApps and best deals & offers). Access to exclusive content only for Jio customers.

question: How to increase the credit limit of my Jio postpaid number?
answer: You can pay an additional security deposit to increase the credit limit of your Jio postpaid number using the MyJio app or visit Jio.com. The amount paid will remain as a security deposit. Please follow the steps below: Using the MyJio app: Log in to the MyJio app with your Jio number and verify the OTP Tap on the ‘Mobile' option from the top header Tap on ‘Increase Credit limit’ under 'Add-ons & payments' section Enter the amount and tap on pay Select the preferred payment option and make the payment to increase the credit limit Using Jio.com: Click here to log in with your Jio number and verify the OTP Go to ‘My account’, click on the ‘Settings’ icon, under ‘Bill Settings’ select the ‘Credit Limit’ option Enter the amount and click on pay Select the preferred payment option and make the payment to increase the credit limit Note:The security deposit (including any enhancement done via self-care) is a refundable amount which will be refunded at the time of Permanent Cancellation of postpaid services/migration to prepaid after adjusting any dues The enhanced security deposit cannot be downgraded or adjusted against the postpaid bill while the number is active The enhanced security deposit amount will increase the assigned and the available credit limit by the same amount. Additional Credit Limit:In case you anticipate higher usage for an interim period, you can make an advance bill payment to continue uninterrupted service. This advance payment will impact your available credit limit. However, this additional payment has to be over and above the outstanding bill amount.

question: What if I miss the monthly recharge due date? Will there be any penalty charges?
answer: No. There is no penalty in case there is a delay on doing the monthly recharge. The new monthly recharge validity cycle will start from the next recharge date. We recommend that you recharge in advance to continue enjoying uninterrupted services. Plans recharged in advance shall remain in queue, without losing benefits of the current plan.

question: What is JioPlus?
answer: JioPlus is the all new Postpaid plans providing the best Postpaid service experience for up to 4 new connections per user. Features and benefits of JioPlus More Value Starting at ₹ 449 per month Additional 3 add-on connections @ ₹ 150 per SIM Total monthly charge of only ₹ 899 for a family of 4 (₹ 449 + ₹ 150 * 3) Effective monthly charge of ₹ 225 per SIM More Data Share data with your entire family No daily data limits Truly unlimited free 5G Data with Jio True 5G Welcome Offer More Benefits: Choice number / Premium content / International Roaming Mobile number of your choice Premium Applications like Netflix, Amazon, JioTV First-ever in-flight connectivity while traveling abroad India calling at ₹ 1 per minute with WiFi calling on international roaming One international roaming plan for 150+ countries More Privilege: No Security Deposit required for Existing mobile postpaid users of other operators Credit card users of Axis Bank, HDFC Bank and SBI Card More Care Priority call-back service by care-specialist on single-click More Convenience Move your existing number to Jio without any downtime Missed call on 70 000-70 000 for free home delivery & activation

question: What is JioPlus Family Plan?
answer: With JioPlus Family Plans, you and your entire family can enjoy the benefits of JioPlus with one single bill and sharing benefits. JioPlus Family Plan brings to you the following advantages: Single Bill for the entire family Flexibility of adding multiple additional members at ₹150/month Data sharing/pooling between family members Get additional 5 GB per additional child in family data pool Free voice calls and SMS for the entire family Pay as you go rate of only ₹10/GB post free data consumption Parent SIM comes with pre-activated ISD, and IR can be activated with just 1 click

question: How to view/download my Jio postpaid bill?

question: How to know the outstanding amount of my Jio postpaid number?

question: How many days before the end of the bill cycle, do I need to place the request for a bill plan change?

answer: Yes, you can simply change your plan by visiting the MyJio app’s plan change section.

question: What should I do if the rider does not arrive at my place within the scheduled time of delivery?
answer: In case rider does not reach your premises within the scheduled time for delivery of SIM, please call the rider on his mobile number provided. You will have received an SMS carrying the details of the assigned rider 40-60 mins before your scheduled delivery slot. In exceptional cases, if your rider is not able to deliver the SIM within the scheduled time period, we will proactively send you an SMS with the reason for non-delivery along with a link to re-schedule the order. If SIM card is not delivered after 3 days, please call 1800 894 9999 for support.

question: How to pay bills for my Jio postpaid number?
answer: You can make bill payments using the MyJio app or visit https://www.jio.com/ . Please follow the steps below: Using the MyJio app: Log in to the MyJio app Tap on the ‘Mobile' option Tap on ‘Pay Bill’ and select the amount option and tap on ‘Pay now’ Select the preferred payment option and make the payment Using https://www.jio.com/ : Click on the ‘Pay Bill’ option Enter the Jio number for which you want to make payment, enter the payable amount and click on ‘Proceed’ to continue Select the preferred payment option and make the payment Note: You can also visit the nearest Jio store and make cash payments against your bill.

question: How to know/increase the credit limit of my Jio postpaid number?
answer: You can know the credit limit of your Jio postpaid number using the MyJio app or by logging into your account on Jio.com. Please follow the steps below: Using the MyJio app: Log in to the MyJio app Tap on the ‘Mobile' option Tap on ‘Increase Credit limit’ under 'Add-ons & payments' section Here you can view your credit limit and pay to increase your credit limit Using www.jio.com: Click here to log in with your Jio number and verify the OTP Click on the ‘Settings’ icon, under ‘Bill Settings’ select the ‘Credit Limit’ option Tap on 'Pay bill' to view your credit limit and pay to increase your credit limit. Note: The security deposit (including any enhancement done via self-care) is a refundable amount which will be refunded at the time of Permanent Cancellation of postpaid services/migration to prepaid after adjusting any dues. The enhanced security deposit cannot be downgraded or adjusted against the postpaid bill while the number is active. The enhanced security deposit amount will increase the assigned and the available credit limit by the same amount. Additional Credit Limit: In case you anticipate higher usage for an interim period, you can make an advance bill payment to continue uninterrupted service. This advance payment will impact your available credit limit. However, this additional payment has to be over and above the outstanding bill amount.

question: Is JioAutoPay mandatory for all postpaid customers?

question: Which segments/groups of people can get security deposit waiver?
answer: Existing non-Jio Postpaid users Credit Cards customers Good credit score

question: Is e-Bill mandatory if I opt for a postpaid plan?
answer: Yes, an e-Bill is mandatory for all Jio postpaid users. You will receive the e-Bill on your registered email id. You can also update your email id by logging into the MyJio app or visit Jio.com .

question: Is there any change in Prime Membership requirement for Jio Phone customers?
answer: There is no change for Jio Phone customers. They will continue to enjoy the benefits of Jio Phone.

question: How to change the tariff plan of my Jio postpaid number?
answer: You can change the tariff plan of your postpaid number using the MyJio app or by logging into your account on Jio.com. Please follow the steps below: Using the MyJio app: Log in to the MyJio app with your Jio number and verify the OTP Tap on the ‘Mobile’ option from the top header Select the ‘View plan’ option Select the ‘Change plan’ option and tap on ‘Buy’ Now tap on ‘Proceed’ and confirm to change the plan Post successful confirmation, the plan will come into effect within the next 24 hours You will also receive a confirmation SMS, once the plan is changed in your account Using Jio.com: Click here to log in with your Jio number and verify the OTP Under the 'My Plans' option, scroll down and select the ‘Change Plan’ option Now tap on ‘Proceed’ and confirm to change the plan Post successful confirmation, the plan will come into effect within the next 24 hours You will also receive a confirmation SMS, once the plan is changed in your account Note: The data balance of your current plan will be forfeited on the bill plan change date and new plan entitlements will be applied to the account.

question: What is a local Aadhaar ID?
answer: Local Aadhaar ID refers to the Aadhaar ID issued in the same state where you are applying for a Jio SIM card. For example, Aadhaar ID issued in Maharashtra will be treated as a local Aadhaar ID for Mumbai, Pune or any other cities/towns/districts within Maharashtra. Aadhaar IDs issued in any other state will be treated as Non-Local Aadhaar in these regions.

question: How to check the unbilled usage of my Jio postpaid number?

Alternatively, click https://www.jio.com/dl/my_bills on your mobile and select ‘Unbilled’ tab Using Jio.com: Click here to log in with your Jio number and OTP Tap on ‘View bill’ You can see your unbilled amount in the ‘Unbilled’ tab.

answer: After completing the online payment, your connection will be activated on priority. Your SIM will be delivered to your doorstep in 48 hours. Activation takes just a few minutes for a new Jio number. If you're porting an existing number, it'll take about 3-5 days.

question: How will I be charged for usage while roaming internationally under Jio Prepaid Pack?
answer: International Roaming (IR) usage charges will be applicable based on the location where you are travelling in, the roaming network used and the kind of usage (Outgoing Local, Call back to India, Outgoing International, Incoming Calls, SMS & Data) Jio IR charges are applicable as per the following two options: Pay Go IR Rates – Standard rates applicable as default for Jio customers IR Packs As per your requirement, you can avail Jio IR Pay Go service or you can purchase an IR Pack. For low to moderate IR usage across all countries, IR Pay Go service is recommended with rates starting as low as Rs.2 per min. For medium to high usage in selected countries, customers can purchase IR Packs. Kindly note, child account customers will have to buy suitable international roaming pack to make usage in international destination and cannot make usage on PayGo rates. For International Roaming (IR) tariff, kindly visit https://www.jio.com/selfcare/plans/mobility/jio-international-roaming-plans

question: I opted for a postpaid plan in the middle of the bill cycle. How much data will I get?
answer: Plan Entitlements (including data) are assigned on a prorated basis for the first bill cycle. From the next bill cycle onwards, you will get the benefits for the complete bill cycle. E.g., if you have taken the connection on the 15th of the month for a plan which is given 2x GB data per month and the bill cycle is the 1st of every month, then the first bill will be generated for 15 days only and you will get x GB data for 15 days.

question: If I order two devices in one order, will I get separate invitation emails for two Jio SIMs?
answer: You will receive only one invitation per order. Once the Rider arrives at your address to deliver the SIM, you can inform him that you will require 2 Jio SIMs. Rider will do the activation process for even the additional SIMs.

question: What are the benefits of International Roaming (IR) Packs- Rs. 1101/Rs 1102 vs PayGo Rates?

question: What if I am unable to use data services in another country under Jio Prepaid Packs?
answer: For Android: You will receive automatic APN Settings through SMS/notification post registering to a foreign network. You will have to install this configuration by clicking the ‘install’ option. If you have not received the SMS, then manually update the APN. Just go to ‘Settings’, select ‘Mobile network’ and tap on ‘Jio SIM’. After that, opt for ‘Access Point Name (APN)’, tap on ‘Add APN’, type ‘Jionet and proceed with ‘Save & Close’. For iPhone: The APN is auto-configured.

question: How do I make calls when abroad with Jio Prepaid Plans?
answer: "To call a local number in the country you are visiting: Simply dial the number as locals do, including the area code (e.g., if in the US, dial 212-XXX-XXXX for New York).To call India or another country while abroad: Use the international format: [+] [Country Code] [Mobile/Landline Number]Example: To call an Indian mobile number, dial +91 followed by the 10-digit mobile number (e.g., +91 98XXXXXXXX).Important: Do not add '0' before the mobile number when using the country code."

question: What settings should I do if I am unable to get a network in 160+ countries which is listed?
answer: You will be able to latch onto the foreign network automatically if your smartphone is on auto network selection. If you are unable to get a network in the country apart from 160+ countries listed in the PDF below, then please follow simple steps related to the settings mentioned below: Click Here for applicable list of countries Settings to be performed if you are in a country apart from 150+ countries listed in the above PDF:--> For iPhone: Step 1 : Go to ‘Settings’, tap on ‘Mobile Service’ and then, select ‘Jio SIM’. After that, under ‘Network Selection’, switch the ‘Automatic’ ON. Step 2 : Go to ‘Settings’, tap on ‘Mobile Service’ and then, select ‘Jio SIM’. After that, use the toggle button to enable ‘Data roaming’. Step 3 : In case SIM does not register to network automatically, select the network manually from the handset ‘Settings’. All you have to do is first switch off ‘Automatic’ from ‘Network Selection’ and then manually select the preferred operator from the list (refer to the list of operators in the visited country). For Android: Step 1 :Ensure that the 'Preferred Network' type is set to GSM/WCDMA/LTE on your mobile device. Step 2 : If the device does not register automatically, then turn off 'Automatic' and select 'Available network’. All you have to do is first switch off ‘Automatic’ from ‘Network Operators’ and then manually select the preferred operator from the list (refer to the list of operators in the visited country). To check the applicable list of countries, please click here.

question: How can I disable Background Data under Jio Prepaid Packs?

If you wish to stop selective apps then only toggle off the desired apps.

question: Will the call be handed over from VoLTE to WiFi calling and vice versa if I am on a Call under Prepaid Pack?

question: Why should I increase my credit limit?
answer: When you opt for Postpaid non-recurring packs from MyJio app or Jio.com, you will get the option to make a payment and increase your credit limit. The amount paid by you remains as a deposit. We recommend that you increase the credit limit at least by the pack amount being chosen by you as it will help you to enjoy uninterrupted services. In case, you do not increase the limit at the time of opting for the pack, you may be asked to make interim payments whenever your credit limit is about to get exhausted.

question: What will happen to unutilized balance of International Roaming (IR) Pack- 1101/ 1102 after validity expiry under Jio Postpaid Packs?

question: How can I activate International Roaming services on my Postpaid connection?
answer: International roaming can be activated in just 3 simple steps via the MyJio app or jio.com MyJio App Open the MyJio App and Tap on ‘Mobile’ icon. Select ‘International roaming’ Click on ‘International Activations’ and click on 'Activate' button to activate International Roaming. You can check your credit limit, or you can enhance it, if you wish to, by continuing the journey. Enter the amount you wish to increase the credit limit by, make the payment. Jio.com Sign in to your account by entering Mobile number Select ‘Settings’ and then ‘Manage service’ Enable ‘International Roaming’ by sliding the button towards the right, and click on ‘Submit’ You will receive a message once International roaming has been activated. You can check your credit limit, or you can enhance it, if you wish to, by continuing the journey. Click on ‘Enhance credit limit’. Enter the amount you wish to increase the credit limit by, make the payment. Note: International roaming services do not work on JioFi and JioPhone devices.

question: Will my International Roaming (IR) service get deactivated post expiry of my International Roaming (IR) pack under Jio Postpaid Pack?

question: I have subscribed to the IR Pack 1101/ 1102 and I have received 5 International SMSs along with this pack, can I use these 5 SMSs while on International Roaming (IR) under Jio Postpaid Packs?
answer: No. The 5 International SMSs offered in IR ₹1101/₹1102 pack can be used for sending International SMSs from India.

question: What are the benefits of Annual IR Pack 2799?
answer: Jio offers the most affordable Annual IR Pack for the top countries at just ₹2799. Here are the benefits of the Annual IR Pack: Unlimited incoming SMS 100 minutes for Incoming and Outgoing calls, thereafter PayGo tariff will be applicable Incoming calls include Wi-Fi Calling, Outgoing calls over Wi-Fi are allowed for call back to India only 2 GB High-speed data and beyond that Fair Usage Policy (FUP) which means once you have exhausted your data quota, unlimited internet at 64 kbps Outgoing calls to local/ROW numbers and SMS over Wi-Fi Calling are not allowed (To make calls/SMS disable only Wi-Fi Calling in your device’s Settings) Rest of the World calls will be chargeable, standard PayGo rates apply Applicable in 50+ countries on select partner and network only. For the applicable country list, click here This pack requires manual activation through MyJio or Jio.com and won't be activated on first use. Go to the upcoming plan section from here to activate. Need to recharge or to know more about IR packs? Please click here

question: If I have taken Postpaid International Roaming (IR) Pack, do I need to deactivate it after I return to India?
answer: No. All our International Roaming (IR) packs are for fixed validity. These expire as soon as the validity is over. You do not need to deactivate the packs at all.

question: When will the International Roaming (IR) Pack s validity start for 1101/ 1102 under Jio Postpaid Pack?
answer: Your pack will remain in queue until you make an International Call/Data/SMS after reaching the foreign location or, if you send an International SMS available in Global IR ₹1101/₹1102 packs while you are still in India.

question: I am a Postpaid customer, how can I activate International Roaming (IR) services?
answer: Steps to recharge International Roaming (IR) through MyJio app Open MyJio app Tap on ‘Go to telecom section’ Scroll down to ‘ISD & roaming’ menu Tap on ‘Int'l Roaming' and click on 'Activate' You will see a message that 'Your request for IR has been submitted successfully along with a Reference Number'. This page will also reflect the credit limit assigned to you You will receive a Notification message once the IR entitlement is successfully registered in your account Steps to Increase Credit Limit via MyJio app: Click on the 'Enhance Credit Limit' option towards the bottom right corner with ₹ symbol Or you can click on 'Purchase an IR Pack' in case you want predefined IR Pack entitlements If you select 'Enhance Credit Limit' then please enter the desired Credit Limit amount and Click 'Pay' (You can Enter the amount in multiples of hundred and thereafter) Please select your desired mode of payment After successful payment, you will see a confirmation message stating 'Your security deposit payment request has been processed successfully'. You will receive a notification message once the security deposit is successfully updated in your account Steps to activate IR services via Jio.com: Open jio.com website Click on ‘SIGN IN' & select ‘Mobile Enter your Jio number and click on 'Generate OTP' Enter the OTP send on your registered mobile number and click ‘Submit’ Click the 'Settings' Icon (Right Hand Corner) Select 'Manage Service' Enable 'IR' by sliding the button towards the Right and Click on 'Submit' You will see a message that 'Your request for IR has been submitted successfully along with a Reference Number' You will get an option to either 'Proceed' further to increase the Credit Limit. In case, you do not wish to increase the Credit Limit, click 'Not Now' option Steps to Increase Credit Limit by paying an additional Security Deposit: Click on Proceed option to Increase the Credit limit Post entering the desired Credit Limit amount, Click 'Update' (You can Enter the amount in multiples of hundred and thereafter) 'Proceed' to pay Please select your desired mode of payment After successful payment, you will see a confirmation message stating 'Your security deposit payment request has been processed successfully'. You will receive a notification message once the security deposit is successfully updated in your account

question: Who can buy this Annual IR Pack?
answer: All Prepaid customers can purchase this IR pack except for J&K, Assam and North East customers. For Postpaid, all circle customers can avail this pack. This Pack is currently not available for JioPhone and Jio Bharat devices.

question: What are the benefits of International Roaming (IR) Packs- Rs. 1101/Rs 1102 vs PayGo Rates under Jio Postpaid Packs?
answer: If the IR service is already activated then you can use IR services provided you have sufficient monetary balance in your account. In such case, you have to recharge with Top Up voucher to get monetary balance. IR Pack Rs. 1101/Rs. 1102 offers monetary balance and any usage made in IR is charged at standard PayGo rates. Also, IR Pack Rs. 1101/Rs. 1102 pack offers 5 International SMS free which can be used to send ISD SMS while in India.

To cancel your port-out request, send an SMS CANCEL followed by a space and the 10-digit mobile number to 1900 from the mobile number for which you have submitted the documents.

question: What if I do multiple recharges of the same Jio Data Booster?
answer: It will behave like 2 paper vouchers that are taken and one is used and the other is put in the pocket, to be used when the first one is used up. Similarly, these digital vouchers will be queued up in your account, for usage only once the first voucher has expired or exhausted.

question: Is the 319 prepaid plan applicable for existing Jio prepaid customers?
answer: Yes, ₹319 prepaid monthly plan is available to both New and Existing customers.

question: What is the 319 Prepaid Plan from Jio?
answer: The ₹319 plan from Jio offers a unique calendar-month validity. This means the plan is valid for one month from the recharge date, and you’ll need to recharge on the same date every month. For example, if you recharge on 5th March, your next recharge will be due on 5th April. Benefits: You don’t need to worry about remembering your plan expiry date It works like a postpaid plan, where your recharge will always be on the same date each month First recharge date Next recharge date Next recharge date Next recharge date 5th March 5th April 5th May 5th June....

question: What happens if I recharge on these dates: 29 Jan, 30 Jan, 31 Jan, 31 Mar, 31 May, 31 Aug, 31 Oct?
answer: In case you recharge on any of the above dates, then your recharge ends on the last day of the next month and your new recharge cycle is: 29 Jan – 28 Feb (In case of a leap year its 29 Feb) 30 Jan – 28 Feb (In case of a leap year its 29 Feb) 31 Jan – 28 Feb (In case of a leap year its 29 Feb) 31 Mar – 30 Apr 31 May – 30 Jun 31 Aug – 30 Sep 31 Oct – 30 Nov

question: What are the benefits of the 319 plan?

question: What are the eligibility criteria for Jio True 5G Unlimited trial through 51, 101 151 addon?
answer: ₹51 Addon Plan Customers having 1.5GB/Day plan and validity up to 1 month, can recharge with ₹51 to get Jio True 5G Plan. ₹101 Addon Plan Customers having 1GB/Day plan, can recharge with ₹101 to get Jio True 5G Plan. Customers having 1.5GB/Day plan and validity > 1 month & <= 2 months, can recharge with ₹101 to get Jio True 5G Plan. ₹151 Addon Plan Customers having 1.5GB/Day plan and validity > 2 months & <= 3 months, can recharge with ₹151 to get Jio True 5G Plan.

question: How can I Upgrade to Jio True 5G in the middle of the month, if I ve missed recharging with 2GB/Day or higher plan?

However, any customer who has opted for a lower data quota plan, can upgrade anytime during the month by simply recharging with ₹51, ₹101, ₹151 and enjoy Jio True 5G plan. 1 GB / day customers ₹101 Add on Plan - Prepaid customer having 1GB/Day plan can recharge with ₹101 to get Jio True 5G Plan. 1.5 GB / day customers ₹51 Add on Plan – customers with plan validity up to 1 month, can recharge with ₹51 to get Jio True 5G Plan. ₹101 Add on plan – customer with plan validity > 1 month & <= 2 months, can recharge with ₹101 to get Jio True 5G Plan. ₹151 plan – customer with plan validity > 2 months & <= 3 months, can recharge with ₹151 to get Jio True 5G Plan. *Validity of the True 5G Unlimited plan is the same as the validity of Active plan

question: What if I have lost or damaged my Jio SIM? What should I do?
answer: In case you have lost or damaged your SIM card, depending on your case, you can follow these steps. Get the broken SIM card replaced: 1. Visit the nearest Jio Store along with your Aadhaar card or any other valid Proof of Identity (POI). 2. A charge of ₹50 will be applicable at the time of SIM replacement. 3. Make sure your Jio number has an active recharge plan during the replacement process. 4. Once your SIM is changed, SMS services will remain unavailable for 24 hours as a security measure. Suspend your lost SIM card: 1. visit the SIM Lost Login page to suspend or resume services on your Jio number. 2. This helps protect your number from misuse until you get your SIM replaced. 3. After suspending the SIM, you can visit a Jio Store to get a new SIM issued. 4. For information on SIM lost or damaged while on International Roaming, click here

question: Which number should I call to complete the tele-verification process for my Jio SIM?
answer: Please call 1977 from your Jio SIM to complete the tele-verification process. If you wish to activate data services only, dial 1800-890-1977 from any number. You can tele-verify your number using any of the following options: Enter the 5-digit PIN received on your alternate number Enter the last 4 digits of your Aadhaar ID Connect with our contact centre advisor for manual verification After successful verification, the services of your Jio SIM will be activated.

question: I want to port my number to Jio. What should I do?
answer: To port your current mobile number to Jio online, you can follow the steps below. Step 1: SMS PORT <10-digit mobile number> to 1900 from your existing mobile number. Step 2: You will receive an SMS that will contain the UPC (Unique Porting Code) and its expiry date. Step 3: Visit your nearest Jio Store or Jio Retailer with the UPC. Step 4: Carry your original Aadhaar Card or original Proof of Address (POA)/Proof of Identity (POI) to place an MNP request. Note: Clear all pending dues with the current operator (if you have a postpaid mobile connection). As per the regulations of the government, if you are porting within the same telecom circle, the number will be activated within 3 working days after successful validation. For porting to another telecom circle, it will take up to 5 working days. Your services will not be interrupted in the intervening period. Port-in requests from the customers of Jammu & Kashmir, Assam, and the North East will be processed within 15 working days. Please note that services will not be interrupted in the intervening period. Ready? Open this link to place a request to port a SIM card number to Jio.

question: How do I manually select a network for Jio Postpaid International Roaming (IR)?
answer: Your device will automatically connect to our partner networks when roaming internationally. Choose from the available partner networks. If the option is not available, contact International Roaming Support on +91 70188 99999 via WhatsApp or call us (Free from your Jio number).

question: Can I Deactivate my Annual IR Pack?

question: What if the country I am travelling to is not covered under the Postpaid International Roaming (IR) packs?

question: How do I make calls to India or local numbers on Jio Postpaid International Roaming?
answer: "To call a local number in the country you are visiting: Simply dial the number as locals do, including the area code (e.g., if in the US, dial 212-XXX-XXXX for New York). To call India or another country: Use the international format: [+] [Country Code] [Mobile/Landline Number] Example: To call an Indian mobile number, dial +91 followed by the 10-digit mobile number (e.g., +91 98XXXXXXXX). Important: Do not add '0' before the mobile number when using the country code."

question: I have subscribed to the Postpaid International Roaming (IR) Pack, and have unused Pack balance what will happen to this balance once I return to India or after Pack expiry?
answer: The pack balance will get forfeited on pack expiry and it cannot be used while in India.

question: I have recharged Postpaid International Roaming (IR) pack. Will I be able to make ISD calls while in India, using the talk time available?
answer: No, the International Roaming (IR) pack works only when you are travelling overseas. To make international calls from India, you can recharge your number with our ISD pack available on MyJio & jio.com or you can make ISD calls using credit limit available.

question: Can I access Jio's media apps while on International Roaming (IR)?
answer: Jio media apps (e.g. JioTV, etc.,) are designed to work only on Jio's 4G & 5G network. These services are not available on International Roaming (IR).

question: What is the new US-Mexico-U.S.V.I pack for postpaid Jio numbers?

Here’s the benefits of these packs: Unlimited incoming calls and SMS Outgoing calls include Local calls within the country and call back to India (wherein call back to India includes Wi-Fi calling as well) Incoming calls can be received form any country including Wi-Fi calling High-speed data and beyond Fair Usage Policy (FUP) which means once you have exhausted your data quota for the day, unlimited internet at 64 kbps Outgoing calls and SMS over Wi-Fi Calling are not allowed (To make calls/SMS disable only Wi-Fi Calling in your device’s Settings.) Standard pay-go rates will be applicable for Rest of the World (ROW) calls Need to recharge or to know more about IR packs? Please click here. Kindly Note: This pack is applicable for US, Mexico and U.S. Virgin Islands only This pack is currently not available for JioPhones and Jio Bharat devices

question: How do I activate International Roaming (IR) on my Jio postpaid number?
answer: Once you recharge, IR services will be automatically activated along with the IR plan as soon as the first usage happens at a foreign location.

question: Can I enjoy the VoLTE experience even when roaming internationally?
answer: Currently, Jio offers an HD calling experience in its VoLTE network However, you can continue using International Roaming (IR) service on international networks by purchasing an IR plan.

question: Do I need to change my phone settings to use International Roaming?
answer: "Most smartphones will automatically connect to the available network. To ensure seamless connectivity, verify these settings on your device: Network Selection Mode: Set to ""Automatic"" Network Mode: Set to ""LTE/4G"" or ""Auto"" Data Roaming: Enable to use mobile data abroad* You can find these options under Settings > Mobile Data (iOS) or Mobile Networks (Android). Note: Data roaming charges apply as per your IR plan. To prevent unnecessary data charges, you may switch off Data Roaming. If you require support while abroad, please message ‘Hi’ to our Global WhatsApp Support at +91 70188 99999 or call our dedicated International Helpline at +91 70188 99999 (Toll-Free for IR users). Our international team will prioritize your call to resolve roaming issues immediately."

question: Will Value Added Services (VAS) like JioTunes and Missed Call Alerts work on International Roaming (IR)?
answer: Yes, Value Added Services active on your Jio number, e.g., JioTunes or Missed Call Alerts will work on International Roaming (IR).

question: I am a postpaid user. How can I activate International Roaming (IR) after reaching another country?
answer: You can buy the IR pack even when you are in an international location. After the pack purchase, your phone will stop receiving signals once the activation process begins. Your phone will have to be restarted for your IR settings to be processed. After purchasing a pack (Prepaid & Postpaid) or for IR Activation (Postpaid), in the below-mentioned countries, if you are unable to register to the network, then follow the below steps: For iPhone: Step 1 : Go to ‘Settings’, select ‘Mobile Service’ and opt for ‘Jio SIM’. Over there, under ‘Network Selection’ you can switch the Automatic on. GGo to ‘Settings’, select ‘Mobile Service’ and opt for ‘Jio SIM’. After that, use the toggle button to enable ‘Data roaming’. Step 2 : In case SIM does not register to network automatically, select the network manually from the handset’s Settings menu. Just go to ‘Settings’, select ‘Mobile Service’ and tap on ‘Jio SIM’. Over there, under ‘Network Selection’, switch off Automatic and select the preferred operator from the list (refer to the list of operators in the visited country). Step 3: In case the SIM still does not register to the network after following Step 1 and Step 2, then switch iPhone off International IMSI. To do that, all you have to do is, go to ‘Home’, select ‘Settings’ and tap on ‘Phone’. Then under the ‘SIM Application’, you can switch off ‘International Roaming’. For Android: Step 1: Ensure that the 'Preferred Network' type is set to GSM/WCDMA/LTE on your mobile device. Step 2: If the device does not register automatically, then turn off 'Automatic' and select 'Available network'. For that, you need to go to ‘Settings’, select ‘Mobile network’ and tap on ‘Jio SIM’. Over there, under ‘Network Operators’, you will be able to switch off Automatic and select the preferred operator (refer to the list of operators in the visited country). Step 3: If still not registered, then look for Jio4G/STK available on the device’s home screen and select STK/Jio4G Menu Within STK/Jio4G, select ‘International Roaming’ and switch it ‘OFF’ After that, wait for 5 minutes and your device will register to the network To check the applicable list of countries, please Click here.

question: What is International Roaming (IR)?
answer: International Roaming is a service which allows you to use your Jio SIM while travelling to another country outside India. With your Jio SIM, you can travel to 160+ countries and enjoy seamless voice, data and SMS service experience.

question: How to know if International Roaming (IR) is activated on my Jio postpaid number?

Tap on the Mobile option 3.Scroll down to view International services section and 4. select International Roaming Activation From this screen, you can easily activate or deactivate your international roaming service Watch this helpful video that walks you through the process! https://youtu.be/JYS7k3O0DHM?si=kwtElKUUg19_I1A3

question: Will a postpaid card of Jammu & Kashmir, Assam and North East work when in International Roaming (IR)?

However, postpaid customers of Jammu & Kashmir, Assam and North East are allowed to use the IR service on PayGo rates. The International Roaming plans with VoLTE services are available in applicable countries.

question: What if I am unable to use data services in another country?
answer: Here’s how you can use data services in another country: For Android: You will receive automatic APN Settings through SMS/notification post registering to a foreign network. You will have to install this configuration by tapping on 'Install' If you have not received an SMS then you can manually update the APN Mobile network -> Access Point Name -> Add APN - > Type “Jionet” -> Save & Close For iPhone: Go to Mobile Data -> Mobile Data Options -> Mobile Data Network -> APN -> Type 'Jionet' to add/save

question: How can I disable Background Data?
answer: Jio postpaid users can disable Background Data by following the steps below: For Android users: Go to Settings > Locate and tap on Data Usage. To stop all apps from running in the background, click on the menu icon on the top right and select 'Restrict Background Data' or locate and tap on the app you want to stop from running in the background and tap on 'Restrict Background Data'. For iOS users: Go to Settings > General > Background Apps > Toggle Off 'Background App Refresh' to stop all apps. If you wish to stop selective apps then only toggle off the desired apps.

question: Will my JioFi/JioPhone device work in International Roaming (IR)?
answer: No, your JioFi/JioPhone will not work when in International Roaming (IR).

question: What settings do I need to check to use VoLTE International Roaming (IR) services on my device?
answer: Follow the below steps to enable VoLTE and explore the HD calling experience. Android Tap on Settings Tap on Network & Internet Tap on Call Tap to enable VoLTE/HD Voice/Enhanced calling iPhone/iOS Tap on 'Settings' Tap on 'Cellular' Ensure the Cellular Data switch is in the 'On' position Tap on 'Voice & Data' Ensure LTE, VoLTE On is selected (has a checkmark)

question: Will the call be handed over from VoLTE to Wi-Fi calling and vice versa if I am on a call using a postpaid plan?
answer: If you are on VoLTE Roaming call, you will continue to be on VoLTE until the VoLTE coverage is available. If you move out of the VoLTE coverage area, then the call will drop. If you are on Wi-Fi Calling, then the call will continue until Wi-Fi Calling coverage is available. If you move out of a Wi-Fi coverage area, then the call will drop.

question: Will my Jio SIM work in all countries with a Jio postpaid plan?
answer: Jio International Roaming service is available in 160+ countries. You can visit our website to check the list of countries where International Roaming service is currently available.

question: How do I check my voice, SMS or data usage on International Roaming (IR) under Postpaid Pack?
answer: You can check your usage details on MyJio or jio.com. Login to your account, and click on the 'Usage' tab to check your usage details. Note: Usage details are not real-time. You might have to check after a few hours for recent 4G data usage details.

question: Can I call on Emergency, Satellite and Special Services Numbers using Wi-Fi Calling Services?
answer: No, Wi-Fi Calling does not allow calls to Emergency, Satellite or Special services numbers.

question: How do I get support for VoLTE issues while on International Roaming?
answer: You can reach out to us via: MyJio App: Go to the JioCare section for quick assistance. WhatsApp Support: Message "Hi" to +91 70188 99999 (Available 24/7). International Helpline: Call our specialized Overseas Support Team at +91 70188 99999 (Toll-Free for International Roaming customers).

question: What are the charges for calls made through the IR 1102 Wi-Fi Calling pack?
answer: The tariff for Wi-Fi Calling from IR 1102 and Global packs are listed below: Outgoing voice calls to India – ₹1/min Incoming voice calls – ₹1/min Incoming SMS – Free Video outgoing and incoming calls – ₹1/min + Data from home data bucket Note: Outgoing SMS, outgoing local voice calls, and outgoing voice call to the rest of the world are not allowed. Wi-Fi Calling service can be availed using Wi-Fi Calling pack only and will not be available for PayGo customers. Sl. question: How to enable Wi-Fi Calling?
answer: You can enable Wi-Fi Calling in three simple steps: Go to Settings and switch on the Wi-Fi Calling feature on your Wi-Fi Calling capable handset Connect to a Wi-Fi network and enjoy Wi-Fi Calling on any Indian number To learn how to turn on Wi-Fi Calling and check the handset eligibility, click www.jio.com/wificalling Note: Some applications can download data even when you are not actively using them. We recommend setting your ‘Mobile Data’ as ‘OFF’ or managing your application settings to avoid this data usage.

question: Will I be able to make Video Calls in International Roaming (IR)?
answer: International Wi-Fi Calling allows you to make video calls on any Jio number. To know more about our Value Plans with Wi-Fi Calling, click here

question: What are the additional charges for using VoLTE International Roaming (IR) service for postpaid?
answer: There are no additional charges for using VoLTE services in International Roaming (IR). The tariff will be the same irrespective of VoLTE or 2G/3G network mode selection. Please check TnCs for VoLTE applicable countries list. Click here: for more details

question: I am registered on the network as a postpaid customer but I am unable to see VoLTE symbol on my device. What shall I do?
answer: There are no additional charges for using VoLTE services in International Roaming (IR). The tariff will be the same irrespective of VoLTE or 2G/3G network mode selection. Ensure you are registered on the applicable partner networks Make sure that VoLTE feature is enabled on your device Please check TnCs for VoLTE applicable countries list. Click here: for more details

question: Whom should I contact if I face issues with my Jio SIM while abroad?
answer: "If you require support while abroad: WhatsApp: Message ""Hi"" to +91 70188 99999 Call: Dial +91 70188 99999 (Free when calling from your Jio number) Our international team will prioritize your call to resolve roaming issues."

question: How do I find out if my device was manufactured before 2019 before traveling to australia ?
answer: You can check your device specifications online by searching for your device's make and model on Google or your manufacturer's website.

question: How will I be charged for calls made through Wi-Fi Calling using the Unlimited IR packs ?
answer: The tariff for Jio Wi-Fi calls using the Unlimited IR pack is given below: Unlimited IR pack come with entitlement benefits - Covers Wi-Fi Calls and Video Calls to India along with existing Cellular IR Usage entitlement in the same bucket of the respective packs Post-Pack Entitlement Charges - Call rates for Call-back to India, post entitlement will be charged at Re. 1/min Incoming Wi-Fi Calls and Video calls to remain Free like cellular incoming calls as per the pack validity IR Wi-Fi outgoing and incoming video calls to Jio Numbers - Video data usage to be deducted from the home data bucket of the customer Jio Wi-Fi IR usage Unit Within Unlimited IR Pack Commercial Usage limit Beyond Commercial usage limit of Unlimited IR pack Outgoing – Calls to India ₹/Min Free (Consumed from pack entitlement) ₹1/min Outgoing - Local Not allowed Not allowed Outgoing - RoW Now allowed Now allowed Incoming calls ₹/Min Free (Consumed from Pack entitlement) Free SMS Outgoing ₹/SMS Not allowed Not allowed SMS Incoming ₹/SMS Free Free *Video Outgoing – Calls to Jio numbers in India ₹/Min Free (Consumed from pack entitlement) + (Video call data usage is deducted from home data bucket) ₹1/min (And Video call data usage is deducted from home data bucket) *₹1/min (And Video call data usage is deducted from home data bucket) ₹/Min Free (Consumed from pack entitlement) (Video call data usage is deducted from home data bucket) Free (Video call data usage is deducted from home data bucket) *For Video Outgoing and Incoming Calls, the pack entitlement will be consumed from the available benefits. question: Why am I unable to make or receive Wi-Fi calls on my Jio Postpaid SIM while abroad?
answer: If you are unable to use Wi-Fi calling on your Jio Postpaid number abroad, check the following: Device Support: Ensure your phone supports Wi-Fi Calling. Software: Update your device to the latest software version. Settings: Go to your phone settings and switch ON "Wi-Fi Calling." Internet: Ensure you are connected to a stable, high-speed Wi-Fi network. Active Plan: Ensure you have an active International Roaming pack that supports Wi-Fi calling. Still facing issues? Global WhatsApp Support: Message "Hi" to +91 70188 99999. Our specialized International Roaming team is available 24/7 to help you get connected.

question: Do I have to activate IR service to be able to use In-Flight services?
answer: No. You only have to recharge with the In-Flight pack to be able to use In-Flight Data, Voice and text services. Your IR service will get activated on recharge of the In-Flight Pack automatically.

question: How do I check if my device supports 000 calls before traveling to Australia?
answer: You can contact your device manufacturer directly, provide your device model number, and check with them to find out if your device supports emergency numbers in Australia.

question: Can I call someone who does not have Wi-Fi Calling enabled on his/her phone?
answer: Yes, you will be able to make calls to any Indian number depending on your plan.

question: Will Wi-Fi Calling work on a prepaid card of Jammu & Kashmir, Assam & North East when in International Roaming?
answer: As per Indian Regulations, IR pack purchases for Jammu & Kashmir, Assam and North East are not allowed on prepaid subscriptions, due to which you will not be able to use Wi-Fi Calling .

question: How can I get support for Wi-Fi Calling issues while abroad?
answer: For support while abroad: Our dedicated Global Help Desk is available 24/7 to assist you. WhatsApp Support: Message "Hi" to +91 70188 99999. International Helpline: Call our specialized Overseas Support Team at +91 70188 99999 (Toll-Free for International Roaming customers).

question: What is Wi-Fi Calling?
answer: Wi-Fi Calling enables you to make and receive calls over a Wi-Fi network using your existing Jio number. The benefits of Wi-Fi calling in International Roaming are listed below: Benefits of Wi-Fi calling in International Roaming: Make and receive voice calls on any Indian number with Wi-Fi using your existing Jio number at just ₹1/min Video call your loved ones on any Jio number Easily connect in case of signal issues in remote areas or dark cellular zones of buildings Wi-Fi Calling doesn’t carry any additional charge. All you need is a Wi-Fi Calling pack and an HD voice-compatible device To learn how to turn on Wi-Fi Calling and check the handset eligibility, click www.jio.com/wificalling Note: The charging for Wi-Fi Calling will be deducted from the respective IR pack balance. Charging for video calls will be deducted from the IR Pack balance and home data bucket. A base plan with sufficient home data balance should be available to make video calls to the Jio number. Grace plan customers will not be able to use Jio Wi-Fi Calling services.

question: I have recharged with an In-Flight Pack. Which all outgoing voice calls I can make?
answer: All voice outgoing calls (except calls to satellite, premium IR numbers, Short codes, and special numbers) are allowed.

question: I have recharged with an In-Flight Pack, when will the pack validity start?
answer: Your pack will remain in queue until you make a call/data/SMS while in the flight. On the first usage instance on the In-Flight Pack, the pack validity starts and remains valid for next 24 hours from that time. Please note In-flight services network will be available only in the covered flight and when your flight attains height of 20,000 feet above the ground.

question: I am an In-Flight Pack user, will I be charged for incoming messages while on In-Flight?
answer: All incoming text messages are free for In-Flight pack users. For non-pack users In-Flight services are not available and hence, incoming messages cannot be received.

question: What happens if my In-Flight Pack gets expired?
answer: In-Flight Pack offers limited entitlements of Data, Voice and text usage. You may choose suitable In-Flight pack as per your In-Flight usage requirements You can also do multiple recharges of In-Flight Pack You can do another recharge of In-Flight pack through MyJio or jio.com before expiry of your existing IFC pack and exhaustion of data entitlement Please note that In-flight services are available only to In-Flight pack users as long as validity and entitlements are available. In-flight services are not available on PayGo rates.

question: What services are currently available on Wi-Fi Calling while roaming internationally?
answer: Wi-Fi Calling pack offers the following benefits: Outgoing calls to any Indian mobile number Incoming calls from India or anywhere in the world Incoming SMS Video calls to any Jio number in India Local calls within the visited country or calls to the rest of the world are currently unavailable Video calls on the non-Jio number and outgoing SMS are not allowed

question: Can I use Wi-Fi Calling while in Airplane mode?
answer: Yes, the Wi-Fi Calling service can be used when your phone is in ‘Airplane Mode'.

question: Can I use IFC Packs while the plane is in Indian Airspace?
answer: No, as per regulation In-Flight Communication (IFC) services are currently not available while you are in Indian Air Space.

question: Why am I facing call mute/call drop issues while using the Wi-Fi Calling service?
answer: Wi-Fi Calling requires a stable Wi-Fi connection. You may experience a call drop issue if you are not within the Wi-Fi coverage area or if the Wi-Fi network quality is poor.

question: Where can I view my In-Flight Packs usage in my Statement of Account / Invoice?
answer: In-Flight Packs usage will be displayed under ‘Itemized Usage’ with the header ‘Satellite’ in your Statement of Account / Invoice.
