question: How can I use Jio IFC Packs and the steps activate IFC Packs?
answer: To use IFC Packs providing inflight data, outgoing voice calls and SMS, you have to recharge with the IFC Packs (applicable for both prepaid and postpaid customers) before boarding the flight when connected to Jio network. Once you are in the flight and the flight has reached 20,000 feet and above, follow these steps: Switch on your phone with Airplane Mode off Your phone will automatically connect to the AeroMobile network. The network name may differ dependent on the handset If your phone does not connect to the AeroMobile network automatically you will need to go to ‘Carrier’ in your phone settings and manually select AeroMobile Ensure Data Roaming is on to use data services Once connected, you will receive a welcome text and pricing information You can now use your mobile phone to call, text, email and surf the internet* Incoming calls are not allowed. You will need to disable Wi-Fi connectivity to use mobile data. As a courtesy to fellow passengers, please keep your phone on vibrate. *Please note - some applications can download data even when you are not actively using them, and you may be charged as a result. Manage your application settings to help avoid these costs. International roaming rates apply.

question: I am a Postpaid customer, can I buy prepaid In-Flight pack?
answer: Yes, you can buy prepaid In-Flight Pack. Steps to purchase In-Flight Pack are given below. MyJio App Open the MyJio app Select Mobile and Tap on ‘Get an Add on packs’ Select ‘In-flight Connectivity packs’ Click on Inflight connectivity packs Select the pack which you want to recharge Tap on buy and select the payment mode and make the payment Jio.com Open www.jio.com Click on ‘Mobile’ then select ‘International Services’ Tab. Scroll and select ‘Plans you may like’ under ‘In-flight connectivity packs’ From the packs available choose – ‘In-Flight Connectivity’ packs Choose your pack as per your need and click on ‘Buy’ Post recharge, your IR services will be automatically activated along with the IR pack. Jio Store/ Jio Retailer You can visit our Jio store or Jio Retailer to recharge your Jio number with our In-Flight Pack Post recharge your IR services will be automatically activated along with the In-Flight pack

question: What is the Data speed I will get in the IFC Packs?
answer: The network speed of In-Flight connectivity is dependent on the in-flight network operators (OnAir or AeroMobile) and the regulations governing air travel. The in-flight network operators are constantly reviewing and innovating their services to provide a better surfing experience for the customers.

question: How can I activate In-Flight Pack?
answer: To use In-Flight pack providing in-flight data, outgoing voice calls and SMS, you have to recharge with the IFC Packs before boarding the flight. Once the flight has reached 20,000 feet, please follow these steps: Switch ON your phone with Airplane Mode Off Your phone will automatically connect to the AeroMobile network (the network name may differ dependent on the handset) If your phone does not connect to the AeroMobile network automatically, you will need to go to ‘Carrier’ in your phone settings and manually select ‘AeroMobile’ Ensure Data Roaming is ON to use data services Incoming calls are not allowed. You will need to disable Wi-Fi connectivity to use mobile data. As a courtesy to fellow passengers, please keep your phone on vibrate.

question: Can I access Jio's media apps while in flight?
answer: Jio media apps (JioTV, etc.) are designed to work only within India. They are not available on international roaming.

question: What is International roaming In-Flight service?
answer: Jio Inflight service allows you to stay connected in the covered flight at above 20000 feet. You need to recharge with Jio prepaid in-flight communication Packs before onboarding the flight. Customers with active In-flight connectivity (IFC) Packs, will be able to use data, outgoing voice calls and SMS (wherever applicable) as per the pack entitlement quota available. Please check the list of airlines/international flights in which you can use the in-flight service here

question: Does Wi-Fi Calling consume data? How much data will a Wi-Fi call consume?
answer: Here’s how Wi-Fi Calling works under Jio postpaid plans: Voice calls are consumed from your IR pack balance and do not consume data Wi-Fi Calling for Video calls consumes data from the home data plan balance The actual data usage of your video call may vary Wi-Fi Calling for video calls is allowed only to Jio numbers in India

question: Will I be charged for incoming calls while using In-flight service?
answer: Incoming calls are not allowed in Jio Inflight service. Only Inflight Data, Outgoing voice and SMS services are available, to the active In-Flight Pack users.

question: What if I am unable to use data services In-flight?
answer: If your phone does not connect to the AeroMobile network automatically you will need to go to ‘Carrier’ in your phone settings and manually select AeroMobile. Step 1: To use data services, you will need to ensure Data Roaming is On Step 2: Once connected, you will receive a welcome text and pack benefits information Step 3: You can now use your mobile phone to call, text, email and surf the internet* *Please note that some applications can download data even when you are not actively using them, and you may be charged as a result. Manage your application settings to help avoid these costs.

question: What happens when Aircraft is closer to the ground?
answer: During take-off or landing, if you have recharged with a normal international roaming pack (₹1101 with unlimited data international roaming pack, etc.) and you are using the internet, the pack may be automatically activated due to low elevation of the aircraft, and strong signals from ground levels. To prevent this from happening, you may wish to turn on flight-mode on your phone during take-off and landing, and when the aircraft is near your destination.

question: I have subscribed to IFC Packs. Will I be able to make Video calls while in the flight?
answer: No, video calling feature is currently not available in Inflight services. However, if you are using any OTT app (like WhatsApp) to make the video call then you will be able to continue to do so provided you have kept the data roaming ON. The data will be consumed from your IFC Pack entitlements.

question: Can I recharge with the IFC Packs after reaching another country?
answer: Yes, you can recharge with the In-flight pack on jio.com/ MyJio by connecting to either a mobile or Wi-Fi network, just before boarding your flight.

question: How do I make calls when in flight, and having IFC Packs activated?
answer: Dial '+' followed by the country code, then key in the phone number. For example, to call from Singapore to India, dial +917018899999

question: How do I get  support for In-Flight Roaming  or network issues while I am in the flight?
answer: If you are connected to the In-Flight network and require support: WhatsApp Support: Message "Hi" to +91 70188 99999. Voice Support: Call our Global Helpline at +91 70188 99999. Important Note on Usage Charges: Voice Calls: Minutes will be deducted from your active In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) Pack voice entitlement. WhatsApp: Data used will be deducted from your IFC Pack data entitlement.

question: How do I check my IFC pack Data, voice and SMS balance entitlements?
answer: You can check your usage details on MyJio or jio.com. Login into your account, click the 'Usage' tab to check your usage details. Note that your usage details are not real time and will have to check after few hours for recent data usage details.

question: How will I get to know that my request for a bill plan change is successfully submitted?
answer: You will receive an SMS confirmation post submitting the request for a bill plan change. A planned change confirmation SMS will be sent once your new plan gets activated.

question: Can I recharge with Top Up packs?
answer: Top Up recharges are not applicable for postpaid customers.

question: Can I recharge with the available ISD/IR packs?
answer: You may recharge with the ISD/IR packs and use the services if there is no pending bill or the credit limit is not exhausted. The ISD service is pre-activated and the IR service can be activated by logging in to Jio.com. You will be able to use the services as per the credit limit available on your Jio number.

question: What is the password for my postpaid e-Bill?
answer: You will receive your e-Bill on your registered email ID. You will require an 8-characters password to open this file. The password for opening the PDF is a combination of the first 4 letters of the name in which your connection is registered (in lower case) as it appears on your Jio postpaid account (ignore spaces in the name if any) and the last 4-digits of your Jio postpaid number. For example: If your name is Ramesh Kumar and your Jio postpaid number is 9XXXXX7007, then your password will be rame7007 If your name is R. M. Kumar and your Jio postpaid number is 9XXXXX7007, then your password will be r.m.7007 If your name is R MKumar and your Jio postpaid number is 9XXXXX7007, then your password will be rmku7007 You can also save the file for future reference or even print copies if needed.

question: How can I register for JioAutoPay on my postpaid connection?
answer: You can register for JioAutoPay via the MyJio app or Jio.com by using a bank account number and authenticating via net banking or debit card.

question: How do I manage family plan?
answer: In a family plan, there must be one parent connection and up to 3 child connections. Customers who have recharged with ₹449 or ₹749 plans will have the option to add remove child. Steps to manage family plan are mentioned below: MyJio Open MyJio and go to My Plan section Click on ‘Manage Family plan’ banner You will see option to ‘Add member’ into family pool Enter child connection number to be added Verify OTP received at child connection Your child is successfully added. 5 GB data/ month will be added in the family data pool. Parent bill will carry an additional charge of ₹150 per month per child plan added in family account. Once a child account is added, you may choose to remove it at any point of time. Jio.com Open MyJio and go to My Plan section Click on ‘Manage Family plan’ banner You will see option to ‘Add member’ into family pool Enter child connection number to be added Verify OTP received at child connection Your child is successfully added. 5 GB data/month will be added in the family data pool (Parent will also get an additional charge of ₹150 per month.) Once the child is added, you may choose to remove at any point of time

question: What will be my postpaid bill cycle and can I change it under regular plans?
answer: The bill cycle is assigned automatically by the system, depending on the activation date. You can check the bill cycle date by logging into the MyJio app or Jio.com. The option to change the pre-assigned Bill Cycle date is not available.

question: How can I share data with Jio Postpaid Plus family members?
answer: To have better control over your family usage, you can transfer data to each child account added to your Jio Postpaid Plus's family plan. Data transfer can be done up to 5 times for each child account. Please follow the steps below: Step 1: Open the MyJio app Step 2: Tap on ‘Share Data’ under ‘Manage Family Plan’ Step 3: Set data for the child account and proceed

question: Will I be charged additional taxes on JioPostPaid plans?
answer: Yes, taxes will be charged on the monthly rental and additional usage charges, if any. Below are the charges applicable for all of India (including Jammu & Kashmir) CGST: 9% SGST: 9% Total applicable GST = CGST + SGST = 18%

question: What is a family plan?
answer: JioPostPaid Plus is a family plan that allows you to enjoy a single postpaid plan for your entire family. Family plans under JioPostPaid Plus enable you to share your data with your family members. In addition to the convenience of a single bill and single payment, you also enjoy additional savings when you opt for a family plan.

question: Will prepaid card of J&K, Assam & North East work when in In-Flight International Roaming under Postpaid Plans?
answer: No. In-Flight service is an International Roaming service. As per prevailing Indian Regulations, Prepaid subscribers of J&K, Assam & North East will not be allowed to use International Roaming service.

question: What are the benefits of the security suite offered in JioPostPaid plans?
answer: JioSecurity is an advanced mobile security app powered by Norton Mobile Security that provides the following benefits: Protection against digital threats like risky apps Controls to safeguard your online privacy Lost or stolen device recovery when you need it Single subscription for up to 10 mobile devices Support for Android and iOS devices.

question: How can I pay for my Jio postpaid number after the service is barred or suspended?
answer: Jio provides you with various options to pay your bills via Jio.com and the MyJio app. You can make payment using debit/credit cards and net banking. You can also visit the nearest Jio store and make cash payments against your bill.

question: How do I add connections to my Jio Postpaid Plus family plan?
answer: You can add a new connection to your Jio Postpaid Plus's family plan in a few simple step: Step 1: Open the MyJio app Step 2: Tap on ‘Manage Family Plan’ from My Plans Step 3: Enter the Jio Number you wish to add to your plan Step 4: Validate using OTP and click on ‘Proceed’ NOTE: In case you want to add a new connection as a child to the family plan, you will be provided with on-boarding codes inside the MyJio app. You need to share these code(s) with our home delivery agent/Jio store official for direct on-boarding to a child plan.

question: What are the call charges to other networks?
answer: There are currently no charges for calls to other networks. All calls for JioPostPaid Plus users are truly unlimited.

question: What are the late fee charges if I pay my postpaid after the due date?
answer: If you are unable to pay by the due date, a late fee of ₹100 or 2%, whichever is higher, will be charged on your next bill.

question: How many members can be added to a family plan?
answer: The number of family members in a family plan depends on the selected plan. Click to know plan details.

question: I have an existing postpaid SIM from a competitor. How do I shift my connection to Jio without changing my current number?
answer: You can retain your existing postpaid number and shift your connection to JioPostPaid Plus through MNP (Mobile Number Portability).

question: How long will it take to add my family members to a single plan?
answer: It takes up to 24 hours to add a connection to your family plan. You will receive a notification once the members have been added.

question: What is the process of Porting my number from other operators?
answer: The following steps need to be followed for porting a number to Jio: In case of store purchase SMS PORT <10-digit mobile number> to 1900 from your existing number which needs to be ported to Jio You will get an SMS that will contain the UPC (Unique porting code) and its expiry date Clear all your pending dues with the current operator Walk into the nearest Jio Store or Jio Retailer with the UPC Carry your original Aadhaar card or original Proof of Address (POA)/Proof of Identity (POI) documents to place MNP request In case of home delivery Schedule a home delivery via Jio.com SMS PORT <10-digit mobile number> to 1900 from your existing number which needs to be ported to Jio You will get an SMS that will contain the UPC code and its expiry date Clear all your pending dues with the current operator Provide UPC to Jio home delivery agent Show your original Aadhaar card or original Proof of Address (POA)/Proof of Identity (POI) documents to place an MNP request Note: As per the regulations of government, if you are porting in within the same telecom circle, the number will be activated within 3 working days after successful validation. For porting into other telecom circles, it will take up to 5 working days. Your services will not be interrupted in the intervening period. Port-in requests from Jammu & Kashmir, Assam, and North East will be processed within 15 working days. Your services will not be interrupted in the intervening period

question: The postpaid connections in my family are under different names and registered addresses. Can I add them to my JioPostpaid Plus family plan?
answer: Existing Jio postpaid customers can be added to a family plan even if they are under different names and registered addresses in the same telecom circle. If your family member is in a different telecom circle, you would have to visit your nearest Jio Store to initiate the process of porting that number through Inter State MNP process. If you want to add non-Jio connections, visit your nearest Jio Store and share identity proof and address proof to initiate porting the connection to Jio. To buy a new connection or to switch with the same number, visit our website

question: Will my number stop working for some time if I opt for Mobile Number Portability (MNP)?
answer: Porting to Jio is a seamless process. There is practically no downtime while transitioning from your current operator to Jio.

question: I have a Jio prepaid connection, how can I get JioPostPaid Plus connection on the same number?
answer: Yes, you can migrate your prepaid number to new JioPlus Postpaid plans yourself. Follow the below simple steps for hassle free migration. Open MyJio App and click on Prepaid to Postpaid migration banner. You will get an OTP on the registered mobile number Enter the OTP and you will see new postpaid plans Select the JioPlus postpaid plan and proceed.

question: Will my family member receive bills for their usage?
answer: No, family plans come with the convenience of one common bill for the entire family. You can check the bill details for all the members in a single bill.

question: I already have a Jio prepaid connection, can I have a postpaid connection as well?
answer: Yes, you can have both Jio prepaid and Jio postpaid connections at the same time.

question: How long does it take to convert Jio Prepaid to Postpaid?
answer: The conversion from Jio Prepaid to Jio Postpaid happens instantaneously.

question: Can my family members change their plans or purchase Data add-on?
answer: Yes, a family member can purchase Data add-on. In case the family member opts for a bill plan change, then they will be removed from the family plans and individual billing will start for that member.

question: What is the benefit of enrolling for JioAutoPay?
answer: JioAutoPay is an automated bill payment instruction given by a customer to Jio. It ensures payments due to Jio are paid automatically every month. You can register for JioAutoPay via the MyJio app or Jio.com by using a bank account number and authenticating via net banking or debit card.

question: Is Home Delivery of SIM available for JioPostPaid Plus plans?
answer: Yes, you can buy a postpaid SIM online and get it delivered to your doorstep.

question: Are there any pre-conditions for mobile number porting?
answer: You should have completed 90 days (from the date of activation of your mobile connection) with your current operator. You should have cleared all outstanding payments with your current operator. You should have no pending request for a change of ownership of the mobile number. Your mobile number you wish to port should not be sub-judice i.e., it should not be involved in any case pending before a Court of Law.

question: Can I add my postpaid JioFi number to my family plan?
answer: Yes, you can add your postpaid JioFi number to your family plan. However, the data benefits of the existing postpaid JioFi plan will cease to exist and the benefits on the primary number can be shared by the dongle number.

question: What is data rollover and how much data rollover is possible in Jio Postpaid Plus plan?
answer: Data rollover means that any unused data in your current bill cycle will carry forward to next bill cycle. Data rollover is not available in the latest Jio Plus Postpaid plans. However, if you were enrolled in an older Jio Postpaid Plus plan, you may still be eligible for the data rollover service. The rollover limit depends on your specific plan and ranges from 200 GB to 500 GB as per below table. Jio Postpaid Plus plan Name ₹449 Plan, ₹699 Plan, ₹949 Plan ₹1199 Plan, ₹1549 Plan Data rollover [Carry forward unused data] Up to 200 GB Up to 500 GB Note: These plans are no longer available for subscription. Existing Jio Plus Postpaid plan customers can recharge with Data packs for additional data requirement as and when required.

question: How can I request for home delivery of a JioPostPaid Plus SIM?
answer: .support-video-wrapper a { color: #000093; } To place a home delivery request, visit Jio.com >> Get Jio SIM >> Home Delivery >> Enter your details. Click to place a request. Home delivery service is currently available in select cities. In case your city is not in the covered areas, you can pick up the JioPostPaid Plus SIM from your nearest Jio store.

question: How can I remove a family member from a family plan?
answer: Follow the steps below to remove a number from your family plan: Step 1: Open the MyJio app Step 2: Tap on ‘Manage Family Plan’ once child accounts are added to the group Step 3: Tap on ‘Remove’ if you wish to remove the child's account Step 4: Tap on the confirmation popup to remove a child account

question: Will all of my family members' data get rolled over while using Jio Postpaid Plus plan?
answer: No, once data is allocated to a family member on your Jio Postpaid Plus plan, it will not be considered for roll-over. Only data available with the main subscriber will be rolled over up to the limit of 200 GB or 500 GB (or as applicable). Note: Data rollover is not applicable in latest Jio Plus plans.

question: How do I track the status of my order?
answer: Click here to track the status of your order. You can also track the status of your order through the MyJio app. Open MyJio >> Visit JioCare > Track Order.

question: How can I define the recurring/default data transfer amount for each member in the JioPostPaid family plan?
answer: Follow the steps below on jio.com and the MyJio app to define the recurring/default data transfer amount for each member in the JioPostPaid family plan: Using the MyJio app: Enter the MyJio app Enter the ‘MyPlans’ section Go to ‘Manage Family Plan’ Tap on ‘Update Percentage’ Input % of total family plan data to be shared (E.g., 0%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, and 25%) Tap on ‘Process’ Using the Jio.com Click on the ‘Sign In’ Option Enter your mobile number and the OTP Click on ‘SELFCARE’ Click on 'My Plans' Select ‘Manage Family plan’ Click on ‘Update Percentage’ Input % of total family plan data to be shared (E.g., 0%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, and 25%) Click on ‘Process’ Note: Data percentage defined will be applicable at the start of the next bill cycle.

question: In JioPostPaid family plan, how to transfer data to child number?
answer: If you have a JioPostPaid Plus family plan, you have three options to manage data transfer. Recurring Plan - You can set a recurring data transfer amount in the MyJio app. Your family members will automatically get the set data amount at the start of every month. You can share 5%, 10%, 15%, or 20% of your total data with individual members every month. You can share up to 50% of your total data with your family members in this plan. One-Time Transfer - You can transfer up to 50% of your total data limit to your family members in a bill cycle. It is not a recurring plan, and you will have to manually transfer data at the start of every month. One-Time Transfer + Recurring Plan - You can choose to share data via the recurring plan and also you can select the one-time data transfer option for your family member. However, the total amount of data shared cannot be more than 50% of your total allocated data in the JioPostPaid Plus family plan.

question: My son/daughter already has a Jio prepaid connection. Can I add him/her to my JioPostPaid Plus family plan?
answer: In case your family member is using a prepaid SIM, the connection needs to be changed to postpaid before you can add him/her to your family plan. To migrate a Jio mobile prepaid connection to Jio Postpaid, simply login to MyJio App with that number, go to Menu, Settings, Service settings and select the option 'Switch to Postpaid'.

question: Can my family member change plans?
answer: In case a family member opts for a bill plan change, he/she will be removed from the family plan and individual billing will start for that member.

question: What is the recurring/default data transfer option in my JioPostPaid Plus family plan?
answer: JioPostPaid Plus family plan allows you to share a specific amount of data with the family members enrolled in the plan. It also allows you to set recurring/default data transfers to your family members using the MyJio app. Once the primary SIM holder decides the total amount of data to be shared with every family member, the system will automatically ensure data transfer to the family members at the start of every bill cycle.

question: How to check the current plan details?
answer: You can check your plan details including data usage and subscription status by simply logging in to the MyJio app. You can find your current plan details and view your bill cycle under ‘Mobile’ >> ‘Current Plan’ >> 'View plan'

question: Is it possible to change the date and time of delivery?
answer: Our home delivery agent would give you a call before visiting the delivery location. Please connect with our agent directly for a revised time of delivery.

question: How much data I can transfer to my family member in JioPostPaid family plans?
answer: You can transfer a total of 50% of your data to your family members in the JioPostPaid Plus family plan, subject to individual limits. You can choose to transfer between 5%, 10%, 15%, and 20% of the total data to your family members, individually. You can send a maximum of only 20% to individual family members under the recurring data transfer plan. If you want to send more than 20% of your total data to a family member, you can only use the one-time data transfer facility. The one-time transfer of data will let you share 50% of your data. However, it is not a recurring activity.

question: How do I contact customer care?
answer: You can easily manage your account & services tasks through the MyJio app, which lets you: Check your plan balance and usage View billing and payment history Pay bills and set up Auto Pay Manage connections like family plans Interact with HelloJio – your voice assistant for all your queries in your preferred language Access FAQs, helpful videos & helpful tips Track complaints and troubleshoot issues To download MyJio app, Click here You can also call us at 199 for any mobile service-related queries

question: Do I need to buy all the SIMs at the same time to get a family connection?
answer: No, you can also purchase SIMs for children at a later date.

question: How do I reach JioCare with JioPostpaid Plus?
answer: Reaching JioCare is now easier than ever. Open the MyJio app to use ‘HelloJio’, the virtual voice assistant. You can also browse through Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) or use the Live Chat option to get answers to your queries. You can download the MyJio app from Android Play Store or iOS App Store. Click here to contact us.

question: Where can I view my past invoices & payment history for my Jio Postpaid number?
answer: You can view your Past bill invoices & Payment history in MyJio App. Steps are as follows: Open MyJio App, Tap on 'Mobile' & scroll down to 'Transactions' section. a. Select 'Payment history' to view your bill payment details b. Select 'Bills & Statement' to view current & previous bills

question: What is the maximum number of times that I can transfer data to a family member in one bill cycle of the Jio PostPaid Plus family plan?
answer: You can transfer data 5 times for 1 family member. Maximum capping of 15 times for all family members in one bill cycle from your JioPostPaid Plus family plan.

question: How can I pay my postpaid bill?
answer: You can pay your bill by logging in to the MyJio app or Jio.com. You can view your bill amount under Mobile >> ‘Bills & Statement’ section >> ‘Overview’ tab. Press on ‘Pay Now’ to pay your outstanding bill. You can pay your Jio postpaid bill using any one of these payment options: Credit card Debit card Net banking e-Wallets UPI You can register for JioAutoPay via the MyJio app or Jio.com by using a bank account number and authenticating via net banking or debit card. You can visit the nearest Jio Store and pay via cash, UPI, credit card, and debit card. You can pay for any of the Jio retailer outlets using Jio UPI.

question: Can I get a hard copy of my postpaid bill?
answer: We are an eco-friendly company and dissuade from printing paper as much as possible. Therefore, we do not send hard copies of the bill. We will mail you the e-Bill that you can print. In case of a necessity, we can help you with a print out at your nearest Jio office (for a TRAI regulated fees of ₹50).

question: What is the timing of your store?
answer: Our stores are open from 10 AM to 8 PM, all days of the week. However, the store timings are subject to guidelines issued by local authorities.

question: How do I track the status of my complaint?
answer: You can check the status of your complaints in MyJio App. Just open MyJio App and tap on JioCare section in the bottom navigation bar of MyJio home page. Select the Track Service Request option in ‘Get Quick support’ section to view the details. Alternatively, click here

question: I have exhausted my monthly data quota in the Jio PostPaid Plus family plan. Can a family member transfer data back to me?
answer: No, a family member cannot transfer additional data to you or any other data member in the case of data quota exhaustion.

question: How does the recurring/default data transfer facility in the JioPostPaid Plus plan benefit me?
answer: With the data transfer facility of the JioPostPaid Plus family plan, you have the convenience of adjusting the data transfer to a family member just once. The plan will automatically transfer the data to the family members in every bill cycle.

question: Is e-Bill mandatory if I opt for this plan?
answer: Yes, an e-Bill is mandatory for all jio Postpaid Plus customers.

question: How can I increase the credit limit of my account for JioPostpaid Plus?
answer: You can pay an additional deposit to enhance the credit limit. Please log in to the MyJio app or Jio.com and select the option to enhance the credit limit. The amount paid will remain as a security deposit. In case you anticipate higher usage for an interim period, you can make an advance bill payment to continue uninterrupted service. Here are the steps to increase credit limit in MyJio: Follow these steps: a. Open MyJio App, Tap on 'Mobile' & scroll down to 'Add-ons & Payments' section. b. Select 'Increase Credit limit' c. Here you can view your current credit limit & choose to increase credit limit by making a payment

question: I want to cancel the recurring/default data transfer facility in the Jio PostPaid Plus family plan. How can I do this?
answer: If you want to reduce the recurring data amount to your family member, then follow the steps below: Using the MyJio app: Open the MyJio app Enter the ‘My Plans’ section Go to ‘Manage Family Plan’ Tap on ‘Update Percentage’ Input % (Set 0%) for selected family members (E.g., 0%, 5%, 10%, 15% and 20%) Tap on ‘Process’ Using www.jio.com: Click on the ‘Sign In’ Option Insert your mobile number and the OTP Click on ‘SELFCARE’ Click on 'My Plans' Select ‘Manage family plan’ Select ‘Share-Plan’ Click on ‘Update Percentage’ Input % (Set 0%) for selected family members (E.g., 0%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, and 25%) Click on ‘Process’ If you want to simply stop sharing the data with a family member, then just enter ‘0%’ while inputting the data amount to be shared, in the steps above.

question: I opted for a postpaid plan in the middle of the bill cycle. How much data will I get?
answer: Plan Entitlements (including data) are assigned on a prorated basis for the first bill cycle. From the next bill cycle onwards, you will get the benefits for the complete bill cycle. E.g., if you have taken the connection on the 15th of the month for a plan which is given 2x GB data per month and the bill cycle is the 1st of every month, then the first bill will be generated for 15 days only and you will get x GB data for 15 days.

question: How will I get my security deposit refunded for terminating the JioPostPaid Plus connection?
answer: If you choose to terminate the connection, you need to settle your last outstanding bill along with any other pending dues, if any. The security deposit will get refunded through bank transfer. Post clearance of dues and disconnection of your number from Jio, you will receive an email/SMS, which will have a deep link, wherein you can update your bank details for faster processing of refund via direct bank transfer. The process of refund usually takes up to 15 days after we receive the required bank details. The link is valid for 7 days from the date it is shared with you. In case the bank details updated by you via the link do not match the bank records, then the direct bank transfer attempt will fail. In such a scenario, you will have to share the NEFT details with us at email ID (Care@Jio.Com)

question: What is the Credit limit for my Jio Postpaid number?
answer: You can view your credit limit in MyJio App. Follow these steps: Open MyJio App, Tap on 'Mobile' & scroll down to 'Add-ons & Payments' section. Select 'Increase Credit limit' Here you can view your current credit limit

question: How can I check my usage/data balance?
answer: Your usage details are available under MyJio >> ‘Mobile’ >> ‘Check Usage’.

question: When is the bill generated for my service and when is the due date for payment?
answer: Your bill is generated on the bill cycle date. You will get 15 days to make the payment after the bill generation date.

question: Why are you asking for security deposit for JioPostPaid Plus?
answer: There is no security deposit for users who have a good credit history/score. Additionally, postpaid users from other operators, porting their numbers to Jio need not pay any security deposit. For other users, a security deposit ensures that they enjoy a host of services including international roaming without any upfront payment.

question: How is Pre-on-Post different from the regular rentals for postpaid users?
answer: For Pre-on-Post, payment is made in advance (like prepaid packs) and these will not affect your overall/monthly bill.

question: Can I recharge and use Pre-on-Post if my postpaid services are barred?
answer: If your bills are overdue and services are barred, you will be prompted to make a bill payment when trying to do a Pre-on-post recharge.

question: How will data entitlement be used when multiple 4G data vouchers are queued along with a Pre-on-Post plan?
answer: To start of data will be used from the postpaid entitlement, then the postpaid add-on packs, followed by the prepaid entitlement, and finally the prepaid 4G data voucher.

question: How to access the Jio PostPaid Plus User Guide?
answer: You can access the step-by-step user guide for all JioPostPaid Plus clicking here.

question: What will be my postpaid bill cycle and can I change it under Jio Postpaid plans?
answer: Bill cycles are assigned automatically by the system depending upon the activation date. You can check the bill cycle date by logging into the MyJio app or Jio.com. The option to change the pre-assigned bill cycle date is not available.

question: Can I switch to Jio without changing my existing mobile number?
answer: Yes, you can port to Jio while retaining your existing mobile number by opting for Mobile Number Portability (MNP) service.

question: What happens in case the JioPostpaid Plus bill is not paid by the bill due date?
answer: Late payment charges start applying if the bill is not paid by the due date. In case of non-payment, outgoing voice calls, data, and other services may get barred. Non-payment of dues may also lead to the termination of the connection.

question: Is the facility available to all postpaid customers of other operators?
answer: Yes, this is available to all existing postpaid users of other operators.

question: What is the benefit of this WhatsApp-based credit limit facility?
answer: The facility lets you join JioPostpaid Plus,and enjoy its rich features without changing your number. You also get a pre-approved credit limit which you enjoyed with your existing operator. Additionally, you do not have to pay any security deposit while porting your number to Jio.

question: What is the process of porting a mobile number to Jio?
answer: Porting your existing number to Jio is a simple and hassle-free process. Online request and FREE Home Delivery: Request a Jio SIM card here Enter Name and Mobile Number Enter OTP Provide plan preference – Prepaid or Postpaid Select ‘Port to Jio’ Enter location details for Jio SIM to be delivered and you are done Our executive will reach out to you to assist with SIM delivery and porting your number to Jio. You can track the status of your request through MyJio app. You can download MyJio here. Please note: SIM Home Delivery is available free of cost. Or Visiting Jio Store: Walk into the nearest Jio Store or Jio retailer outlet with a valid identity and address proof. Trying to locate a Jio Store near you? Find here.

question: Can I recharge with Pre-on-Post plan if I have exhausted my credit limit?
answer: If you have exhausted your monthly credit limit, you will be prompted with a message to make a payment of the bill or security deposit before trying to do a Pre-on-Post recharge.

question: Can I recharge and use Pre-on-Post if my postpaid services are barred due to an outstanding amount?
answer: In case a postpaid number is barred due to non-payment of previous dues, you will not be able to recharge with or use Pre-on-post entitlements. Also, the validity of the pre-on-post plan will not get changed due to payment-related barring.

question: What are Pre-on-Post Plans?
answer: Pre-on-Post is a feature from Jio that allows postpaid customers to enjoy the benefits of prepaid recharges on their postpaid numbers. The best part is that these recharges work regardless of your billing cycle, so you get the full benefits (and not pro-rata benefits). Click here to recharge.

question: Are COCP customers eligible for Pre-on-Post recharges?
answer: Yes, Jio Corporate/Enterprise customers can avail of the Pre-on-Post benefits.

question: What are the documents and processes required to get the Credit Limit facility?
answer: All you need is a recent postpaid bill from your existing operator (the bills should not be older than 2 months). Below are the steps to avail of this offer: Share a copy of your recent postpaid bill on WhatsApp no 88501-88501 from the number you intend to port (please ensure the same number is mentioned on your postpaid bill). You can upload a PDF copy of the bill or an image of the bill. Ensure that uploaded copy is legible and has details like your name, phone number, credit limit, billing details, etc Once uploaded, our team would validate your document. If all the details match, you would get a success message on SMS/email. Your pre-approved credit limit would be pre-configured and set in the Jio system against your existing number You can walk into any Jio store to get a Jio postpaid connection or port your existing number to Jio. Kindly carry your KYC document (preferably your Aadhar Card) You do not have to pay any fee or security deposit while switching to Jio

question: Are there any charges for porting my number to Jio?
answer: Porting your number to Jio is absolutely FREE. There are no home delivery charges, no activation charges, and no security deposit.

question: What is the benefit of the WhatsApp based credit Limit Facility for porting to Jio Postpaid?
answer: WhatsApp-based Credit Limit facility lets you join JioPostPaid Plus and enjoy its rich features without changing your number and at the same time, you get a pre-approved credit limit which you enjoyed with your existing operator. Additionally, you do not have to pay any security deposit while switching your mobile number to Jio.

question: Where can I do Pre-on-Post recharges from?
answer: Pre-on-Post recharges are available on Jio.com, MyJio, and any of the Jio Stores, Reliance Digital or Digital Xpress (Dx) Minis which sell prepaid recharges.

question: How to generate a Unique Porting Code (UPC)?
answer: You can generate a Unique Porting Code (UPC) by sending an SMS: PORT(Space)Mobile Number to 1900.You will receive the code via SMS from 1901. Please note that the code is valid only for 4 days. *Please note: The validity of the Unique Porting Code for Jammu & Kashmir and North-eastern states is 30 days.

question: Can I carry forward my existing Credit Limit while porting to Jio Postpaid?
answer: Yes, Jio provides a new WhatsApp-based facility that helps you transfer your postpaid Credit Limit from the current operator to Jio.

question: What are the documents and processes required to avail of the Credit Limit transfer facility?
answer: To transfer the Credit Limit, you just need your existing operator’s recent (not older than 2 months) postpaid bill. Here are the steps to process it: Share a copy of your recent postpaid bill on WhatsApp number (88501-88501) from the mobile number which you intend to port and is mentioned on your postpaid bill. Upload either a PDF copy of the bill or an image of the bill. Ensure that the copy uploaded is legible and has details like your name, phone number, credit limit, and billing details. Once uploaded, our team will verify your document. If all the details match, you’ll receive an update via SMS. Your pre-approved Credit Limit would be pre-configured in the Jio system against your existing number. You can walk into any Jio Store to get a Jio Postpaid connection/SIM with your KYC document/s (Aadhaar card preferred) and your number will be ported to Jio. You can also place an online request for porting and we will have your new Jio Postpaid SIM home delivered at no cost.

question: What are the documents required to get a new SIM?
answer: Following original documents can be shown as Proof of Identity (POI) and Proof of Address (POA) : Aadhaar, Voter ID & Passport. For eKYC based Jio SIM activation process, Only your biometric finger impression and Aadhaar details is required.

question: What are the benefits of porting to Jio?
answer: Porting to Jio opens a whole new world of benefits. Some of the key benefits include: Jio True 5G network provides an unparalleled combination of coverage, capacity, quality, and affordability compared to other networks. Faster data speeds and better voice quality owing to pan-India 4G network Prepaid plans providing maximum value for money Unlimited voice calls to anywhere in India and across any network Complimentary access to Jio's bouquet of apps – JioTV, JioNews, JioGames, etc. Access to premium third-party OTT services on select prepaid and postpaid plans

question: I did not receive SMS containing any rider details after I placed my order for the home delivery of Jio SIM. What should I do now?
answer: You will generally receive the SMS carrying the rider details 40-60 minutes prior to the scheduled time of your Jio SIM delivery. However, in rare situations, if you receive no such SMS, the rider will contact you before visiting your premises for the delivery.

question: What are the charges applicable for a new SIM card?
answer: .support-video-wrapper a { color: #000093; } Getting a Jio SIM is completely free. However, you need to activate an applicable plan at the time of collecting the SIM card. Get Free SIM Home Delivered Click here.

question: What are the current Jio Postpaid and Prepaid Plans?
answer: Jio Prepaid Plans: Phttps://www.jio.com/selfcare/plans/mobility/prepaid-plans-list/ Jio Postpaid Plans: https://www.jio.com/selfcare/plans/mobility/postpaid-plans-list/

question: Will my mobile number become inactive while porting to Jio?
answer: You may experience a few minutes of number disconnection, about which you will be duly informed. As per the standard process, porting is carried out at night, so that service disruption is minimum.

question: How long does it take to port the existing mobile number to Jio?
answer: Mobile Number Portability (MNP) request placed within the same state/circle takes 3 working days and 5 working days for a different state, post sharing the Unique Porting Code (UPC) and valid documents (Proof of Address and Proof of Identity) with our executive. *Please note, the porting process for Jammu & Kashmir and North-eastern states takes 15 days as per regulatory norms.

question: How do I pay for my SIM purchase?
answer: You can pay the JIO representative using cash or credit/debit card.

question: How do I reschedule the home delivery of my Jio SIM?
answer: To reschedule your Jio SIM delivery, please contact the delivery person/rider directly. You will receive a new link where you can choose a new time slot. Please note, that you will be able to reschedule your home delivery only once.

question: How do I convert the current Jio Prepaid to Jio Postpaid connection?
answer: Yes, you can migrate your prepaid number to Postpaid plans yourself. Follow the below simple steps for hassle free migration. Open MyJio App Tap on Menu, Settings, Service settings and select the option ‘Switch to Postpaid’ You will get an OTP on the registered mobile number Enter the OTP and you will see new postpaid plans Select the JioPlus postpaid plan and proceed.

question: What documents do I need to port my mobile number to Jio?
answer: You need a Unique Porting Code (UPC) and valid Proof of Identity (POI) and Proof of Address (POA) document such as Aadhaar, Voter ID, Passport, or Driving License. Alternatively, you can also use the eKYC facility. Check the steps to generate Unique Porting Code here.

question: Do I need any offer code to get SIM home delivered?
answer: Yes, the rider who will be visiting your home on behalf of Jio to issue the Jio SIM will ask you for an offer code. If you have purchased a JioFi, the IMEI of the device will be your offer code. IMEI is usually printed on the box & on the inside of the battery space of the device. If you have purchased a 4G smartphone, please download MyJio App, click on ‘Get Jio SIM’, enter your details & generate the coupon code. Please note that only one SIM can be issued on a single offer code.

question: Is there any additional requirement while porting a postpaid connection to Jio?
answer: In case you wish to port your postpaid mobile number from any other operator to Jio, make sure that the latest bill outstanding plus any unbilled amount with the current operator is paid and there is no contractual obligation in your current number. Additionally, your number should be at least 90 days old with the current operator. Once you raise a porting request online, our executive will call you and explain the process in detail. You can also walk into the nearest Jio Store and get the details about the porting process. However, carry valid identity and address proofs, such as Aadhaar, Voter ID, Passport, or Driving License, when you are visiting our store. To locate a Jio Store near you, check here.

question: What is the new tariff for Jio Prepaid mobile customers?
answer: Reliance Jio has unveiled new tariff applicable for its prepaid mobile customers. These changes are effective from 3rd July 2024, 00:00 hrs. For tariff details, click here.

question: What will happen to my active prepaid mobile plan?
answer: Your active prepaid plans will continue to work as is. Upon expiry of current active plan validity, you will have to purchase new plan from any channel like Jio.com, MyJio, TPA, Jio store or Jio retailer.

question: Why should I switch from 4G to 5G?
answer: India's only True 5G network provides an unparalleled combination of coverage, capacity, quality, and affordability compared to other networks. Here’s how 5G benefits will revolutionise your experience: Standalone 5G architecture: World's most advanced 5G network with zero dependencies on 4G network Low latency, machine-to-machine communication, 5G calls, edge computing, and network slicing Far superior to 4G-based non-standalone networks being launched by other operators Largest and best mix of spectrum: Largest and the most appropriate mix of wireless spectrum for 5G across 700 MHz, 3500 MHz, and 26 GHz bands, giving Jio True 5G a distinct advantage over other operators Jio is the only operator that has a 700 MHz low-band spectrum to ensure deep indoor coverage Carrier Aggregation: Seamlessly combines 5G frequencies into a powerful 'data highway' using advanced technology This will enable you to enjoy 5G speed, up to 1 Gbps with ultra-low latency, and the best indoor and outdoor experience.

question: Will recharge plans change for JioBharat & JioPhone users?
answer: There is no change in the tariff of JioBharat & JioPhone plans.

question: Do I get unlimited 5G in my prepaid mobile plan?
answer: To avail Unlimited 5G Data benefit, recharge with a ₹349 or above plan that offers 2GB / day data. If you are using a 5G-compatible device, have an eligible plan, and are in a Jio True 5G coverage area, you can use unlimited 5G data.

question: What are the new applications offered by Jio?
answer: Building on Jio’s core principle of leveraging the power of technology to deliver the best value and services to its users, Jio Platform is introducing two new applications: 1. JioSafe - Quantum-secure communication app for calling, messaging, file transfer and more (priced at ₹199 per month). 2. JioTranslate - AI-powered multi-lingual communication app for translating voice call, voice message, text and image (priced at ₹99 per month). Jio users will get both these applications (worth ₹298/month) absolutely free for a year.

question: How long does it take for a new SIM card to get activated?
answer: It may take anywhere from a few hours to a couple of days for the SIM to get activated depending on whether it is a new number or a ported number. You will be informed of this duration after you place the order.

question: What is 5G ?
answer: 5G is the 5th generation mobile network which is the latest in mobile telecommunication technology. Jio is deploying the most advanced version of standalone 5G, which has zero dependencies on the existing 4G network. Other operators are deploying a non-standalone version of 5G that operates on their 4G core, making Jio True 5G far superior With standalone 5G, Jio can deliver new and powerful services such as low latency connectivity, massive machine-to-machine communication, 5G calls, edge computing, network slicing, and the metaverse.

question: When is Jio True 5G launching in my city/area?
answer: Jio True 5G has now covered most of India! Open this link to check all the places across India where True 5G is available.

question: Will my Jio postpaid monthly plan rent change?
answer: There is a change in postpaid mobile plans as well. If you are an existing customer, monthly rent of will be revised to new tariff upon completion of 6 months from the date of activation. Customers can also change the plan through MyJio / Jio.com in case they want to continue on a different offer Login to your account, and go to 'My plan' option to change your plan.

question: Have a 5G-compatible handset and my city has Jio 5G network. Am I eligible for the Jio True 5G plan?
answer: The Jio True 5G plan applies only to users with 5G devices, located in cities where Jio True 5G services are available and have a valid active prepaid (more than 2GB/Day) or postpaid rental ₹199 or above Plan. Prepaid customer has below 2GB/Day plan can upgrade with below Add on recharges. 1 GB / day customers ₹101 Add on Plan - Prepaid customer having 1GB/Day plan can recharge with ₹101 to get Jio True 5G Plan. 1.5 GB / day customers ₹51 Add on Plan – customers with plan validity upto 1 month, can recharge with ₹51 to get Jio True 5G Plan. ₹101 Add on plan – customer with plan validity > 1 month & <= 2 months, can recharge with ₹101 to get Jio True 5G Plan. ₹151 plan – customer with plan validity > 2 months & <= 3 months, can recharge with ₹151 to get Jio True 5G Plan.

question: Will I be able to use 5G on international roaming?
answer: 5G services will soon be available on International Roaming through partner operators abroad.

question: What is Jio s 5G Wi-Fi service?
answer: To enable 5G-for-all, we are launching Jio True 5G-powered Wi-Fi services in high-footfall areas such as educational institutions, religious places, railway stations, bus stands, commercial hubs, and other locations. We have already launched True 5G-powered Wi-Fi services in the temple town of Nathdwara, Rajasthan to help users to experience Jio’s True 5G service first-hand. True 5G-powered Wi-Fi services are already available in Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga, Shri Mahakaal Mahalok, Guruvayur Temple and Maa Kamakhya Temple premises. We will keep you informed as we cover more locations with Jio True 5G-powered Wi-Fi services.

question: How do I check if my device is Jio5G compatible?
answer: Click here to select your device's brand and model to check its Jio True 5G compatibility.

question: What are the new ISD Minute Packs?
answer: Jio has launched 7 new value-for-money, affordable ISD Minute Packs. These packs offer ISD minutes entitlement to popular ISD-calling countries.

question: What will happen to the prepaid mobile plans in queue?
answer: Prepaid plans in queue will continue to give the same benefits that the plan is providing pre-3rd July 2024. Upon expiry of the active plan, the queued plan will get activated with the eligible benefit applicable as on plan recharge date. Example: If a user recharges with ₹239 unlimited plan that provides 1.5 GB/day data and unlimited 5G data before 3rd July 2024 and this gets activated post 3rd July once the existing plan validity is over, then the user will get 1.5 GB/day data and unlimited 5G data benefit.

question: I did not receive SMS with OTP on my mobile number while trying to schedule the home delivery of Jio SIM. What should I do now?
answer: In case you do not receive SMS with OTP on your mobile number you can re-try with some other mobile number. We would use this registered mobile number for all future communications.

question: Will my paired smartwatch work if I upgrade to Jio 5G on my smartphone?
answer: Yes, your paired smartwatch will work seamlessly.

question: What are the benefits of Carrier Aggregation?
answer: Carrier Aggregation is an effective tool to extend the coverage of mid and high bands that leads to an increased capacity. Jio is uniquely positioned to use this feature as it has a spectrum in the low, mid and high bands to provide an exceptional True 5G experience.

question: Will I be able to pair my smartwatch with my smartphone which is working on Jio 5G?
answer: Yes, it can be paired easily.

question: Do I need to upgrade from a 4G SIM to a 5G SIM? Will Jio True 5G work with eSIM? How does a 4G SIM convert to 5G?
answer: No, you don’t need to upgrade. All Jio SIMs, including eSIMs, are already 5G-ready. Just insert your existing SIM into a 5G-enabled phone or activate a Jio eSIM and recharge with an eligible Jio True 5G plan to get started.

question: I am buying a new 5G device. Do I need to check whether it supports SA?
answer: All new 5G smartphones available in Indian markets are compatible with Jio5G SA network. For best Jio True5G experience, please ensure your mobile software is updated to the latest version and 5G is selected as preferred network in device settings.

question: Where can I buy a True 5G-compatible handset?
answer: You may visit Reliance Digital Store or Jio Store for the latest range of 5G devices. Alternatively, you may also visit online JioMart or reliancedigital.in sites/app. You can avail of exciting offers and financing options on these devices. We will continue to notify you of the exciting 5G handset offers.

question: What are the bands on which Jio 5G is available?
answer: Jio 5G is available on the n28, n78, and n258 bands, making it one of the leading providers when it comes to 5G bands in India.

question: What happens if I move from a 5G coverage area to a 4G coverage area?
answer: With Jio, you will be able to move seamlessly between 5G and 4G networks without any action or intervention required on your part. The network display on your mobile will show 5G or 4G depending on the coverage available in your location.

question: Does Jio have any 5G phones?
answer: All JioPhone models currently available in the market are 4G compatible.

question: Can I buy a Jio5G Dongle? Will my JioFi work on Jio 5G?
answer: All JioFi models currently available in the market are 4G compatible.

question: Can I transfer 601 True 5G upgrade gift voucher?
answer: Yes. This voucher is transferrable. You can transfer it to any other Jio prepaid customer.

question: What is 601 Jio True 5G upgrade gift voucher?
answer: The new ₹601 Jio True 5G upgrade gift voucher is digital gift voucher that entitles Jio 5G handset users to upgrade to unlimited 5G for 12 months. The ₹601 voucher contains 12 X 5G upgrade vouchers worth ₹51 each. This ₹51 voucher can be redeemed on monthly mobility plans of 1.5 per day entitlement. A Jio user can purchase this gift voucher for himself or his/her friends / loved ones.

question: Can I transfer the individual 51 vouchers to my friends / relatives?
answer: No, the ₹51 voucher is not transferrable. However, you can transfer the ₹601 Jio True 5G upgrade gift voucher to your friends & relatives before they redeem it on their Jio number.

question: How do I redeem the 51 voucher?
answer: The ₹51 voucher can be redeemed on monthly mobility plans of 1.5 per day entitlement. To redeem follow these steps: Login to MyJio app and scroll down to My Voucher section. Tap on 'Redeem' icon in My Voucher section. Tap on 'Redeem' at the bottom to complete the process. Confirm redemption of the voucher. Your voucher has been successfully redeemed.

question: Can I redeem 601 5G upgrade gift voucher on my number?
answer: Yes. You can redeem the ₹601 True 5G upgrade gift voucher on your number as well. On redemption, you will get 12 vouchers of ₹51 in your MyJio account. This ₹51 voucher can be redeemed on monthly mobility plan of 1.5 per day entitlement.

Enter the requested details to create your Youth Pass Users can receive an invitation from other Youth Pass holders (We encourage existing Youth Pass customers to share the Youth Pass with as many of their friends as possible for them to enjoy the benefits as well.) Once the Youth Pass is generated, applicable benefits can be accessed instantly through the MyJio app Customers who are not on the Jio network can simply port to Jio or get a new Jio SIM to access the Youth Pass benefits

answer: The complimentary voucher will be auto credit in ‘Voucher’ section of MyJio once you insert and activate your new prepaid Jio SIM in your new device during the offer period. Customer has to redeem the voucher from the voucher section to avail the benefits.

question: Can I transfer the Jio 5G Bonanza Offer to any other Jio number?
answer: Jio 5G Bonanza Offer is non-transferable. Once credited, it will work on the same Jio SIM on which the offer has been provided.

question: How will I get to know if the offer Plan Jio 5G Bonanza Offer has been credited to my account?
answer: You will be notified through SMS/ Email once the Jio Jio 5G Bonanza Offer is credited to mobile number. You can view the Plan details in Myjio App and Jio.com as well.

question: I am not a Jio user and purchased a 5G device. How do I get the Jio Jio 5G Bonanza Offer?
answer: Customer can avail the offer after porting his/her number in Jio Network within offer period to get benefits of Jio 5G Bonanza Offer. The customer has to port to within 7 days of purchase of the new device.

question: How can I get Unlimited 5G data offer on my Jio SIM?
answer: If you're looking to unlock 5G unlimited data with zero hassle, Jio makes it effortless. Since Jio runs on a true 5G-ready network, you don’t need a new SIM to enjoy 5G SIM unlimited data or unlimited 5G speeds. Your existing SIM is already future-ready. If you’re on a 2GB/day or above plan, you automatically get unlimited 5G data. But even if you’re on a lower daily data plan, you can upgrade yourself to unlimited data 5G at any time by choosing an add-on and instantly activating Jio True 5G unlimited benefits. If you use a 1.5GB/day plan ₹51 Add-on: If your plan validity is up to 1 month, recharge to get unlimited 5G data. ₹101 Add-on: If your plan validity is >1 month & ≤2 months, recharge to enjoy 5G network unlimited data. ₹151 Add-on: If your plan validity is >2 months & ≤3 months, recharge to activate 5G internet unlimited benefits. If you use a 1GB/day plan ₹101 Add-on: Recharge to unlock 5G phone unlimited data instantly. Note: Your unlimited 5G benefits stay active for the duration of your current base plan.

question: Is the Jio 5G Bonanza Offer applicable for existing Jio subscribers as well?
answer: The offer is applicable only on new Jio SIM prepaid connection that are activated during the offer period along with a new 5G device.

question: What is the process of activating Amazon Prime membership after activating JioPostPaid Plus Plans?

question: Can I use two Jio SIMs in the Smartphone which comes under JioExclusive Program?
answer: You can use two Jio SIMs in the Smartphone, both the SIM slots are VoLTE enabled. Data will work on one SIM at a time, with the option to swap data on any one SIM.

question: What is the time period for which the smartphone will be under JioExclusive Program ?
answer: Smartphone purchased under JioExclusive Program comes with a period of 30 months from the time of activation.

question: Can the customer opt out of JioExclusive Program?
answer: Yes. You can opt out of the “JioExclusive” Program before the expiry of Offer period of 30 months by paying applicable admin charges. There are no charges if you decide to opt out of the offer after 30 months. Please follow the below steps (to opt out at any point of time) Step 1: Open MyJio and Go to Settings→'Unlock Device' option Step 2: Accept T&C and make applicable admin charges through available mode of payment options. After successful payment confirmation, you will be successfully migrated out of “JioExclusive” Program within 72 hours.

question: Can I use data over Wi-Fi on device bought under JioExclusive Program?
answer: Yes, you can access data over Wi-Fi on devices bought under “JioExclusive” Program provided you have a Jio SIM with valid recharge plan in the device.

question: How many smartphones can I buy under JioExclusive Program?
answer: You can buy as many smartphones as you want. But you are eligible to buy ONLY one smartphone against one Jio SIM, under the “JioExclusive” Program.

question: What will be the behavior on Jio network of Smartphone bought under JioExclusive Program?
answer: Scenario Device Behavior SIM 1 – Jio SIM 2 – Other Operator The data will work on Jio SIM which will be primary SIM irrespective of slot. The other operator SIM, voice & SMS will work as long as there is an active Jio SIM with valid recharge plan in other slot. SIM 1 – Jio SIM 2 – Jio The data will work on both the Jio SIMs, basis selection.

question: Can I sell my smartphone registered under JioExclusive Program to any other customer?
answer: Yes, the customer will have to use an active Jio SIM in any one of the SIM slots .

question: What are the requirements to enroll under JioExclusive Program?
answer: You need to have an active Jio SIM with a valid recharge plan. In case you don’t have a Jio SIM, you can activate a new Jio SIM from the authorized retailer or migrate your number to Jio network under MNP policy You can visit your nearest Jio Store, Reliance Digital or Jio retailer to get a new Jio SIM. To locate your nearest Jio Store, click https://www.jio.com/Jio/portal/storeLocator

question: What if my Jio number doesn t have a valid plan on the device bought under JioExclusive Program?
answer: You will get recharge reminders from Jio to recharge your device on time. If your Jio SIM does not have a valid recharge, your device will only be able to make emergency calls and Wi-Fi pairing only to get the device unregistered from server. You will able to use full features of the smartphone when you perform a valid recharge on your Jio SIM.

question: What if my MNP process fails under JioExclusive Program?
answer: Jio SIM is necessary to continue to avail benefits under “JioExclusive” Program , therefore MNP process may be reinitiated or new Jio SIM may be inserted in the smartphone

answer: Yes, “Jio Tecno Spark Go Offer 2021” is available for all (Tecno Spark Go 2021 smartphones, purchased from stores as well as ecommerce sites in India during the offer period.

question: Is the Jio Asus Zenfone Data Offer applicable to existing Jio subscribers as well?
answer: Yes, the offer is applicable for both existing as well as new Jio subscribers.

question: Can I avail benefits under JioExclusive Program if my MNP is under progress?
answer: After successful ORN (Order reference number) generation, you will be eligible to avail the benefits.

question: Can the cashback voucher, under the Jio Asus Zenfone Data Offer be transferred to any other Jio number?
answer: No, the cashback vouchers are non-transferable.

question: Where can I get a new Jio SIM to avail the Jio Asus Zenfone Data Offer ?

question: Is the Jio Asus Zenfone Data Offer applicable on all the variants of 4G devices?
answer: No, it is applicable only on the Indian variants of Asus Zenfone Lite and Zenfone Max (M1).

question: What is the validity of the cashback voucher under the Jio Asus Zenfone Data Offer?
answer: The Cashback vouchers are valid till 31st May, 2022

question: I am currently availing a device cashback offer on my phone. Can I also avail the Jio Asus Zenfone Data Offer on the same phone?
answer: No. If you are already availing an earlier cashback offer of RJIO/RRL on an eligible device, you will be ineligible to participate or avail this Jio cashback offer.

question: What is the eligibility criteria for availing the Samsung Galaxy S10 Jio Double Benefit Offer 2019 ?
answer: You must fulfill the following conditions to be eligible for the offer: You must have purchased an Indian version of the Samsung Galaxy S10+ or Samsung Galaxy S10 or S10e mobile device. You must have latched on Jio network with the eligible device or after 11th April 2019. You must be enrolled to Jio Prime membership. You must have performed a recharge of Rs.4999 prepaid plan offered by RJIO within the offer period.

question: Can I avail any other Jio offer along with the Samsung Galaxy S10 Jio Double Benefit Offer 2019 ?
answer: No, this offer cannot be clubbed together with any other offer of similar nature.

question: How and when will I get the Jio additional recharge coupon and double data under the Samsung Galaxy S10 Jio Double Benefit Offer 2019 ?
answer: The additional recharge coupon and data voucher will be provided automatically on your eligible mobile device after performing recharge of Rs.4999 prepaid plan offered by RJIO. The subscriber will receive an additional recharge coupon and data voucher which needs to be redeemed through MyJio app. Such additional recharge coupon not redeemed within two years and double data voucher not redeemed within one year from the date of credit of such voucher shall expire and be forfeited.

question: Will the Jio Asus Zenfone Data Offer be available, if I purchase a 4G device from ecommerce sites?
answer: Yes, “Jio Asus Zenfone Data Offer” is available for Asus Zenfone Lite and Zenfone Max (M1), purchased from stores as well as ecommerce sites in India, during the offer period.

question: What Samsung Galaxy models are eligible for the Samsung Galaxy S10 Jio Double Benefit Offer 2019 ?
answer: The offer is applicable on Samsung Galaxy S10+ or Samsung Galaxy S10 or Samsung Galaxy S10e mobile devices. The offer is applicable only on the Indian version of Eligible Device.

question: How long is the Samsung Galaxy S10 Jio Double Benefit Offer 2019 valid for?
answer: The offer is valid for Samsung Galaxy S10+ or Samsung Galaxy S10 or Samsung Galaxy S10e activated on the Jio network on or after 11th April 2019 by the eligible subscriber.

question: What is Samsung Galaxy S10 Jio Double Benefit Offer 2019 ?
answer: “Samsung Galaxy S10 – Jio Double Benefit Offer – 2019” entitles you to an additional recharge coupon upon performing a recharge of Rs.4999 prepaid plan for all Jio customers (existing and new) who purchase Samsung Galaxy S10+ or Samsung Galaxy S10 or Samsung Galaxy S10e on or after 11th April 2019. Such additional recharge coupon offers the same benefits offered under the recharge of Rs.4999/- prepaid plan of RJIO as on 11th April 2019 and includes the benefit of double data which will be automatically applied on the eligible device.

question: Can the additional recharge coupon and/or double data under the Samsung Galaxy S10 Jio Double Benefit Offer 2019 offer be transferred to any other Jio number?
